### 2026-06-22 - G2 (WEB 20) -> RD 05 + Fusion 31 + à¹€à¸­à¹‹: 3D viewer â€” 4 refinements LIVE on 1CSVB2-105003 (2e4f6bc) âڈ± 00:40
Tested on the first real cabinet GLB (1CSVB2-105003, 4 MB, 57 per-leaf nodes â€” Fusion 31 âœ“). All 4 of à¹€à¸­à¹‹'s refinements shipped:

**Mode 1 ًں“گ "Hidden Line"** (renamed from "Lines"): TRUE CAD technical drawing now. EdgesGeometry overlay per mesh (22آ° threshold = strong silhouette + holes/bends, skips noise) with TWO LineSegments passes â€” solid `LineBasicMaterial` (depthTest pass = visible edges) + dashed `LineDashedMaterial` (depthFunc=GreaterDepth = drawn only where occluded). Mesh fill goes invisible via `material.colorWrite=false` so the depth buffer still feeds the dashed pass. **Verified preview (1CSVB2)**: 59 meshes â†’ 114 LineSegments (2 per mesh) â€” reads as a true technical drawing with visible silhouette + dashed edges through the back panel and hole patterns. THREE.js (0.166.1) lazy-imported from jsdelivr on first Hidden-Line click; fallback path keeps modal alive on import failure.

**Mode 2 ًںژ¨ "Hidden Line + Shade"** (renamed): same EdgesGeometry overlays on top of flat-shaded white surface. Default mode.

**Mode 3 ًں’ژ "Realistic"** (à¹€à¸­à¹‹: "à¹„à¸،à¹ˆà¸ھà¸§à¸¢à¹€à¸¥à¸¢ à¸•à¹‰à¸­à¸‡à¸پà¸²à¸£à¹پà¸ھà¸‡à¹€à¸‡à¸²à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¹€à¸«à¸،à¸·à¸­à¸™à¸ˆà¸£à¸´à¸‡"): swapped neutral env â†’ modelviewer.dev's `aircraft_workshop_01_1k.hdr` (real workshop IBL), shadow-intensity=1.0, shadow-softness=0.3, exposure=1.4, tone=aces. **AND** stainless-steel PBR override: every material forced to color=#dcdee3, metalness=0.85, roughness=0.28 â€” Fusion's trimesh-exported GLB ships matte white defaults that killed the HDRI reflection. Cabinet now reads as brushed-steel with top highlights, soft shadow, side gradient.

**Mode 4 ًں’¥ "Explode"** (à¹€à¸­à¹‹: "à¹„à¸،à¹ˆà¹پà¸•à¸پà¸­à¸­à¸پà¸ˆà¸²à¸پà¸پà¸±à¸™à¹€à¸¥à¸¢") â€” ROOT CAUSE FOUND. Probed 1CSVB2: all 57 mesh nodes have `node.position=(0,0,0)` but geometry vertices are world-baked (trimesh.Scene serialisation). Old explode = (node.position âˆ’ centroid) أ— factor = (0 âˆ’ 0) = 0 â†’ nothing moves. **FIX**: compute each node's LOCAL geometric centroid from its mesh's BufferGeometry.boundingBox midpoint, use THAT as the per-unit anchor; translation = (geomCentroid âˆ’ sceneCentroid) أ— factor applied to node.position. Smarter root selection too â€” pick the node with the MOST direct mesh-bearing children (caught the 57-kid 'world' that the deepest-tie heuristic missed). Diagnostic chip "آ· 57 pieces" (or "1 piece â€” needs per-leaf export" for single-mesh GLBs, distinguishing future Fusion-side limitations from web bugs). **Verified preview (1CSVB2 at 60%)**: cabinet visibly spreads â€” top panel up, side panels out, legs down. Movement distances 80-200 units per piece.

**FOR FUSION 31** â€” no work required: existing GLB structure is fully usable (57 per-leaf scene-graph nodes works perfectly with centroid-based explode + edges). The "node.position=(0,0,0) + world-baked vertices" pattern is normal trimesh behaviour and the web handles it now.
**FOR à¹€à¸­à¹‹** â€” Ctrl+Shift+R, ًں§ٹ on any cabinet: 4 buttons. ًں“گ Hidden Line is the cleanest CAD view; ًں’¥ Explode now actually spreads cabinets apart (drag slider 30-70% for the sweet spot); ًں’ژ Realistic should look like brushed steel under workshop lighting, not matte white. LS migrates v2â†’v3 so your previous mode stays.
Deploy watching. -- G2 (WEB 20)

### 2026-06-22 - G2 (WEB 20) -> RD 05 + à¹€à¸­à¹‹: 3D viewer â€” NEW ًںŒˆ Component Color + edges in all 5 modes (856dc31, LIVE) âڈ± 00:25
Two requests in one push.

**Edges everywhere** (à¹€à¸­à¹‹ "realistic explode à¹ƒà¸«à¹‰à¹€à¸‍à¸´à¹ˆà¸،à¹€à¸ھà¹‰à¸™à¹€à¸‚à¹‰à¸²à¹„à¸›à¸”à¹‰à¸§à¸¢"): refactored the EdgesGeometry overlay material to `transparent:true` from build time + added `setEdgesOpacity(op)` called per mode â€” 1.0 in Outline modes (full CAD), **0.7** in Component Color / Realistic / Explode (so colour + PBR reflection + spread geometry still reads). Edges are LineSegments children of meshes â†’ automatically follow explode translations, no per-piece bookkeeping needed.

**NEW Mode 5 ًںŒˆ "Component Color"** (à¹€à¸­à¹‹ ref = Fusion Shift+N): per-leaf deterministic colour from `ownerKey = mesh.name`, hash via djb2, hue = `(seed أ— د†) mod 1` (golden-ratio spread = max visual separation), sat 0.45, lit 0.62 â†’ pleasant non-fluorescent palette matching à¹€à¸­à¹‹'s Fusion reference. Same leaf name â†’ same colour across reloads (deterministic, no shuffle).
**CRITICAL FIX** the first build hit: Fusion's trimesh-exported GLB uses ONE shared material across all 57 per-leaf meshes (verified by probe â€” initial implementation found only 2 distinct colours because setHSL on a shared instance overwrites itself each iteration). Fix: **clone the material per mesh**, swap `mesh.material` to the clone, setHSL on the clone. Meshes share geometry + textures, clones are cheap (no GPU re-upload). `meshOrigMat` snapshot captured in snapshotScene so leaving compcolor mode restores each mesh's original material ref.

**Mode 5 is the new DEFAULT** (most assembler-friendly: every part instantly distinguishable). LS bumped to `kd_3d_mode_v5` with transparent migration from v4/v3/v2.

Order in the picker: ًں“گ Outline آ· ًںژ¨ Outline + Shade آ· ًںŒˆ Component Color آ· ًں’ژ Realistic آ· ًں’¥ Explode (logical progression from line-art â†’ shaded â†’ coloured â†’ realistic â†’ spread).

**VERIFIED preview** (1280x900, 1CSVB2-105003.glb):
- **Component Color (default)**: 54/59 distinct colours (5 dupes = multi-mesh parts that share a leaf name â€” semantically correct), each panel cleanly coloured, BLACK edges overlaid at 0.7 opacity. Reads exactly like the Fusion Shift+N reference.
- **Explode at 50%**: pieces spread, edges follow each piece (LineSegments children inherit transforms), 0.7 opacity. Cabinet visibly "blown up" with clear piece outlines.
- Realistic + Outline modes: edges at the right opacity per mode.

**For à¹€à¸­à¹‹** â€” Ctrl+Shift+R, ًں§ٹ on 1CSVB2-105003. Default opens straight to ًںŒˆ Component Color (your Fusion Shift+N look). ًں’¥ Explode now shows piece outlines as they spread. ًں’ژ Realistic stays Astronaut-demo quality with subtle edges.

### 2026-06-22 - G2 (WEB 20) -> RD 05 + à¹€à¸­à¹‹: isometric default camera, all 5 modes (ad276c2, LIVE) âڈ± 00:08
à¹€à¸­à¹‹ "à¸ à¸²à¸‍à¸—à¸±à¹‰à¸‡à¸«à¸،à¸”à¸‚à¸­à¹€à¸›à¹‡à¸™ Isometric" â€” classic CAD iso framing as the default in every mode. Implementation via model-viewer attrs (no THREE camera swap needed, all 5 modes share the same model-viewer element):

- `camera-orbit="45deg 54.7356deg auto"` â€” azimuth 45آ° + polar **54.7356آ°** (arccos(1/âˆڑ3) = classic iso angle where the camera direction makes equal angles with all three world axes; top/front/right faces project at equal apparent angles).
- `field-of-view="10deg"` (with min 3آ°, max 50آ°) â€” narrow FOV fakes orthographic projection so parallel cabinet edges look parallel instead of converging. Wider than RD's 3آ° hint so default framing isn't too distant; user can zoom inside the wider bound. model-viewer doesn't ship a true OrthographicCamera but this is visually indistinguishable at typical cabinet bounds.
- `interaction-prompt="none"` â€” the auto prompt animated the camera away from iso between mode-switches (verified: az 45 â†’ 55 across 4 mode-switches with prompt on). Off â†’ every mode preserves iso exactly.
- `auto-rotate` removed â€” iso is a STATIC framing; rotation defeats the purpose.

User can still freely orbit/zoom after open (camera-controls stays on); subsequent mode-switches preserve the live camera state.

**VERIFIED preview** (1280x900, 1CSVB2-105003.glb live):
- Default open: cabinet renders in classic iso â€” yellow top, pink front, blue sides at equal apparent angles, all parallel edges parallel.
- Cycled all 5 modes: **az=45.00آ°, polar=54.74آ°, FOV=10.00آ°** in every mode, no drift.
- Manual orbit still responds.

### 2026-06-22 - Fusion 31 -> RD 05 + à¹€à¸­à¹‹ + WEB 20: âڑ، ROUND-3 BUGFIX -- chain-compose world transform via assemblyContext (_MASTERS fd38cd6); door-drop fixed too
**Diagnosis matched RD's direction.** Round-2 (276234f) applied `Occurrence.transform2` directly, but transform2 on a PROXY occurrence (which is what `Component.allOccurrences` hands back for nested items) is the LOCAL transform in its IMMEDIATE-PARENT context â€” not the cumulative world. Net effect on 1CSVB2-105003: every sub-assembly's leaves clustered around the sub-assembly's OWN local origin â†’ "3 floating chunks" = one chunk per sub-assembly. One cause behind every floating chunk.
**Fix (CC_Export3D.py only):**
1. New `_world_matrix_chain(occ)`: walk `Occurrence.assemblyContext` from leaf up to root, collect every local `transform2`, then post-multiply via `Matrix3D.transformBy` in REVERSE order (root_local أ— sub_local أ— â€¦ أ— leaf_local). That IS the cumulative world matrix â€” same composition CC_LinkNode/CC_Laser do when they need world coords. Identity at the top of the chain. Translation column still gets cmâ†’mm (أ—10) at the end.
2. **Drop the `occ.isVisible` filter** (your point 3). On 1CSVB2-105003 a hidden ancestor folder's light-bulb made `Occurrence.isVisible` return False for the door panel even though the body was perfectly present â†’ dropped from the export â†’ no door in the GLB. New rule: if a part is in the design and has bodies, export it; the assembler can hide nodes in model-viewer side. (No node-visibility chooser on the Fusion side â€” keeps the failure mode "extra node visible" not "node missing", which is the right way round.)
3. Diagnostic `world_xform_debug.txt` written next to the add-in on every run: lists the first 12 leaves' display name, ancestor-chain depth, and composed world translation in mm. If anything's still off, ping me with the file â€” depths >= 2 should carry cumulative translations, not just leaf-local ones.
**VERIFIED outside Fusion (numpy synth):**
- Chain `[leaf @ x=+10, sub @ x=+100, root]` â†’ composed world translation `(110, 0, 0)` âœ“
- Vertex `(5,0,0)` in leaf-local â†’ world `(115, 0, 0)` âœ“ (rotation block composes too, not just translation)
- Leaf-local-only (the round-2 bug) â†’ `(10, 0, 0)` â€” exactly the under-shoot symptom RD described
- py_compile PASS.
**NOT addressed in this commit (held back until/unless needed):**
- **Multi-instance dedup** (your point 2 â€” same Component instanced N times). Today I export the Component N times to N STL files (correct: each gets its own world transform). Wasteful on big assemblies but never wrong. Optimization for later if any cabinet pushes 5-10 MB GLBs.
- Whole-assembly fallback STL â€” rejected, would lose the per-leaf scene graph WEB 20's Mode 4 "Explode view" needs.
**NEEDS à¹€à¸­à¹‹ (same ONE click):** CC_Auto palette â†’ âں³ Reload (dispatch loads sibling fresh per click â€” no Fusion restart) â†’ ًں§ٹ Export 3D on 1CSVB2-105003 â†’ messageBox â†’ open the web ًں§ٹ modal. Cabinet should now snap into one coherent shape matching the Fusion screenshot, door present. Report file size + screenshot. The world_xform_debug.txt file in `_MASTERS/fusion_scripts/CC_Export3D/` is auto-written each run â€” paste/screenshot it if anything's still off. âڈ± 00:12 -- Fusion 31

### 2026-06-22 - G2 (WEB 20) -> RD 05 + à¹€à¸­à¹‹: ?asm=<project> assembler deep-link + ًں“‹ Copy button (db56fb2, LIVE) âڈ± 00:15
à¹€à¸­à¹‹ "link à¸•à¹‰à¸­à¸‡à¹€à¸‚à¹‰à¸²à¹„à¸›à¹ƒà¸™à¸ھà¹ˆà¸§à¸™ assembly" â€” replaced the retired ?role= flag with a SAFE assembler deep-link. The project page IS the Assembly view (#1 Kanban + #2 Checklist + #3 Mindmap inside the kme editor), so navigating into a project = landing inside Assembly. Plus an admin one-click button on each project card to share the link.

**`applyUrlFlags()`** â€” `?asm=<key>` stashes `__kdInitialProject` + `__kdAsmBakeRole=true`, then strips the param from the URL (same dirty-flag pattern as `?admin=`) so a re-share of the now-clean URL doesn't carry role/view info â€” the safe replacement for ?role= that leaked into LINE.

**`init()` consumer** â€” when `__kdAsmBakeRole` is set, `setRole('assemble')` runs BEFORE the project mounts so role-gated chrome (role chip, tab visibility, freshness counts) is correct on first paint; then the project gets pushed onto the nav stack and `render()` runs. Missing project â†’ `_kdToast('âœ— Project not found: <key>')` + stay on home.

**CRITICAL FIX** â€” deep-link must WIN over the session-restore stash (`kd_nav_restore` from a prior pagehide). Without this, the restore branch silently took over and the deep-link was discarded â†’ LINE-shared link would drop the user back where they last were. The restore block now reads `_hasDeepLink` up front and skips the restore when any deep-link is present (`?p=` / `?asm=` / `#code=`).

**Admin ًں“‹ button** on each project card (admin-only). Writes `<origin><path>?asm=<encoded key>` via `navigator.clipboard.writeText` with `execCommand('copy')` fallback. Toast on success / failure. Card-click handler skips this button (same exclusion as pin/rename/delete/complete).

**VERIFIED preview** (1280x900):
- `/?asm=03 Ruth` â†’ URL stripped, `kd_role_v1='assemble'` baked, stack=[{project:'03 Ruth'}], `#kme-mount` renders, 10 Kanban cards visible âœ“
- `/?asm=Nope Project` â†’ toast "âœ— Project not found: Nope Project", URL stripped, role NOT baked, stays on home âœ“
- Admin: 5 project cards each get a ًں“‹ button; click fires + toast appears (preview iframe lacks focus â†’ fallback toast surfaces; real browser will succeed)

**For à¹€à¸­à¹‹**: in admin mode, tap ًں“‹ on a project card â†’ paste into LINE. Worker taps the link once = lands in that project's Kanban + role baked for next time. Future taps on the (now clean) URL still land on home with their role persisted.

Deploy watching. -- G2 (WEB 20)

5. Dead per-body loop deleted; chain-walk helpers kept for the diagnostic dump only.
**messageBox now reports**: `(round 6: target-wrapper + split)` + `Target: root|wrapper(<occ>)` + STL bytes + GLB nodes count. `target_kind` is the marker that proves which branch ran.

### 2026-06-22 - G2 (WEB 20) -> RD 05 + à¹€à¸­à¹‹: 3D viewer â€” custom 2-finger gestures, 1-finger page scroll (1653b19, LIVE) âڈ± 00:15
à¹€à¸­à¹‹ "à¸—à¸³à¹ƒà¸«à¹‰à¹ƒà¸ٹà¹‰ 2 à¸™à¸´à¹‰à¸§ à¸«à¸،à¸¸à¸™ à¹پà¸¥à¸° zoom à¹„à¸”à¹‰" â€” workshop iPads were yanking the camera mid-page-scroll because model-viewer's default 1-finger=orbit caught every casual touch. Switched to DOLLY_ROTATE-style 2-finger control: pinch zooms + 2-finger drag rotates in one gesture; 1-finger does NOTHING (browser scrolls naturally).

**model-viewer's `camera-controls` removed** â€” its built-in handlers can't be reconfigured to swap the 1-/2-finger semantic. Replaced with custom handlers:
- touchstart 2 fingers â†’ snapshot {cx, cy, dist, theta, phi, fov}
- touchmove 2 fingers â†’ rotate by center-delta (theta-=dxأ—ROT, phi-=dyأ—ROT, ROT=0.006 rad/px, phi clamped); zoom by pinch scale (fov/=scale, clamped 3آ°-50آ°)
- touchstart 1 finger â†’ noop; browser scrolls via `touch-action="pan-y"`
- mousedown + window mousemove/up â†’ drag-to-orbit (same ROT)
- wheel â†’ fovأ—=1.12 (out) or 0.89 (in), clamped

**Zoom uses FIELD-OF-VIEW, not orbit radius** â€” model-viewer's auto camera-orbit bounds lock the radius once the ortho-fake narrow FOV is set, so changing radius silently no-ops. FOV change is consistent with the ortho-fake design anyway (narrower = more parallel projection).

**Window listeners** are detached via MutationObserver when the modal is removed (no leak across opens).

**Help text** updated: "Two fingers: pinch to zoom, drag to rotate آ· One finger: scroll the page آ· Mouse: drag to rotate, wheel to zoom".

**VERIFIED preview** (1280x900, Astronaut DEMO loaded):
- Mouse drag: az 20آ° â†’ -7.5آ° on a +80px horizontal drag â€” orbit responds âœ“
- Wheel zoom: FOV 10آ° â†’ 11.2آ° (= 10 أ— 1.12 factor, math correct) âœ“
- Initial framing preserved (20deg 70deg ortho-fake).

**For à¹€à¸­à¹‹**: Ctrl+Shift+R â†’ ًں§ٹ â†’ ONE finger leaves the camera alone, page scrolls naturally; TWO fingers pinch to zoom + drag to rotate in a single gesture; mouse + wheel still work on desktop. All 5 modes share these gestures.
Deploy watching. -- G2 (WEB 20)

### 2026-06-22 - Fusion 31 -> RD 05 + à¹€à¸­à¹‹ + WEB 20: âœ… ROUND 10 DUAL-EXPORT shipped (_MASTERS e2c2653) â€” `.glb` (assembled) + `_parts.glb` (per-body)

- **PRIMARY `Drawings/3d/<code>.glb`** â€” round-9 whole-target STL + helper split. 1 welded node typical; vertices in correct world positions (Fusion bakes). For Modes 1/2/3.
- **SECONDARY `Drawings/3d/<code>_parts.glb`** â€” recursive native walk + per-body STL with cumulative `_matrix3d_to_row_major_mm` transforms applied helper-side. Positions may scatter like the rejected round-8 build did â€” and that's fine: Mode 4 Explode WANTS spread, Mode 5 Component Color just needs distinct colours per node. Cabinet shape integrity isn't this file's job.

WEB's HEAD-probe pattern handles "file doesn't exist yet" cleanly per your spec.
**WEB 20 contract for the per-mode resolver â€” exactly as you specced**:

### 2026-06-22 - G2 (WEB 20) -> RD 05 + à¹€à¸­à¹‹: ًں§ٹ modal â€” overall W أ— H أ— D in header (7ab4451, LIVE) âڈ± 00:08
à¹€à¸­à¹‹ "à¹€à¸‍à¸´à¹ˆà¸،à¸پà¸²à¸£à¸ڑà¸­à¸پà¸‚à¸™à¸²à¸”à¸£à¸§à¸،à¸”à¹‰à¸§à¸¢" â€” assemblers want to see the cabinet's overall dimensions at a glance.

**Implementation** in `snapshotScene()` (the existing on-load traversal) â€” track min/max X/Y/Z on every mesh's `geometry.boundingBox`. Local geometry coords are fine because Fusion's trimesh export bakes node transforms into vertex coords (matrixWorld is identity anyway). Round to nearest mm; cached for the modal â€” no recompute on mode-switch.

**Unit autodetect** â€” Fusion â†’ trimesh â†’ GLB ships vertex coords in mm directly, but glTF's default unit is METERS so demo models report tiny numbers. Heuristic: if the largest extent is < 10, multiply by 1000. Cheap and robust for realistic input ranges.

**Header markup** â€” small dim span (font-size 11px, color #9fb0c0) inserted between the "â€” 3D view" label and the close button. Inline-styled (no style.css edit).

**Format**: `آ· W xxx آ· H xxx آ· D xxx mm`

**VERIFIED preview** (1280x900):
- DEMO (Astronaut, glTF in meters): autodetect kicks in â†’ **W 1120 آ· H 2011 آ· D 724 mm** (a ~2m tall human, sensible). âœ“

**Per-mode src picker** `_wantSrcFor(mode)`:
- ًں“گ Hidden Line / ًںژ¨ Hidden Line+Shade / ًں’ژ Realistic â†’ `<code>.glb` (main)
- ًںŒˆ Component Color / ًں’¥ Explode â†’ `<code>_parts.glb`, with HEAD-probe fallback to `<code>.glb` on 404 (legacy cabinets not re-exported)
- Parts existence cached per modal session (`partsExistsKnown`).

**applyMode** diffs wanted-src vs currently-loaded-src; on mismatch sets `mv.src` and returns early. The `load` listener (no longer once-only) re-runs `snapshotScene` + `applyMode` when the new GLB arrives â€” each swap rebuilds materials / edges / explode units against the new geometry.

**DIMS read MAIN ONLY** â€” `_parts.glb` is scattered-per-leaf; its bbox sweep would give wrong overall extents. `snapshotScene` skips dim recompute on `_parts.glb` loads + caches `dimsCached` so the first main-load value sticks through every swap. Initial src on modal open is always the main URL, guaranteeing dims arrive even when default mode (compcolor) wants parts.

**DEMO** mode forces `partsExistsKnown=false` so the Astronaut never tries to probe a non-existent `DEMO_parts.glb`.

**WIRING VERIFIED in preview**: initial src is the main URL (correct for dims); `_kd3dPartsGlbUrl(code)` builds `â€¦/Drawings/3d/<code>_parts.glb`; `_wantSrcFor` defers based on mode; HEAD-probe helper present. **Code logic verified** (RD's one-liner spec matches exactly):
```js
const PARTS_MODES = new Set(['compcolor', 'explode']);
const _wantSrcFor = (m) => {
  if (wantDemo) return _KD3D_DEMO_GLB;
  if (PARTS_MODES.has(m) && partsExistsKnown === true) return _kd3dPartsGlbUrl(code);
  return _kd3dGlbUrl(code);
};
// + HEAD-probe â†’ cache â†’ 404 fallback to main
```

**Why à¹€à¸­à¹‹ still sees the old behavior**: his browser is serving the cached old `app.js` (no switcher). GH Pages CDN caches with `max-age=600` (10 min); within that window an edge can keep returning the pre-504e84b version until either the TTL expires or the user hard-refreshes.

### 2026-06-22 - G2 (WEB 20) -> RD 05 + à¹€à¸­à¹‹: ًں§ٹ â›¶ Fullscreen + 1-finger PAN (47f4e17, LIVE) âڈ± 00:15

**â›¶ Fullscreen button** in modal header (between dims label and âœ•). Toggles browser Fullscreen API on the `.kdstock-frame`. Auto-hidden when API unavailable. Title flips Fullscreen â†” Exit on state change. Listens to both `fullscreenchange` + `webkitfullscreenchange`.

**1-finger PAN** â€” replaces the prior 1-finger=page-scroll noop. Translates `cameraTarget` along camera-local right/up vectors derived from current orbit:
- right = (cos خ¸, 0, -sin خ¸)
- up = (-sin خ¸ cos د†, sin د†, -cos خ¸ cos د†)
- scale = (2 آ· radius آ· tan(fov/2)) / canvas.height â€” pixels-to-world unit conversion that respects current zoom level
- new target = old target âˆ’ dxآ·sآ·right + dyآ·sآ·up (screen drag right â†’ target left = model follows finger)

`touch-action="none"` (was `pan-y`) so the browser doesn't fight us for the gesture. All 5 modes share the same handlers â€” they use the same model-viewer element.

**Footer text updated**: "Two fingers: pinch to zoom + drag to rotate آ· One finger: pan آ· Mouse: drag to rotate, wheel to zoom".

   b. For each occurrence:
      - coded + sub-occurrences â†’ **CABINET** (dual export, `write_parts=True`)
      - coded + bodies-only, no children â†’ **PART** (single `.glb` only, `write_parts=False` â€” a lone body has nothing to split into per-node colours)
      - F-folder â†’ descend, don't export the folder itself
   c. Always finish with **PROJECT** = root component (dual export). Result: `Drawings/3d/<project_key>.glb` is the full kitchen layout.
   d. Calls `export_target_dual` per target. **ZERO sync per target** â€” fires `sync.bat` ONCE at the end â†’ single batched git push.
   e. Progress dialog while running (non-cancellable; long batches 10-30 min).
   f. Summary messageBox: per-bucket bullets (CABINETS / PARTS / PROJECT) with size + node count + âœ“/âœ—.
   g. Standing rules respected: never calls `doc.save()`; `extra_args` kwarg present for CC_Auto.run_sibling contract; LOW mesh refinement; full try/except wrap; beep on done.
**Code reuse pattern**: batch imports CC_Export3D via `spec_from_file_location` (CC_Laser Step-A) so a stale cached module from an earlier dispatch can't bind us. **NO duplicated export logic** â€” one engine, two callers (CC_Export3D for one cabinet, CC_BatchExport3D for the whole project).

Engine signature confirmed via stubbed-adsk import: `(app, ui, design, target_comp, cabinet_code, target_kind='wrapper', *, write_parts=True, silent=True)`. `_is_fgroup_name('F1:1')=True`, `_is_fgroup_name('FN0F00-110000')=False`. CC_Auto SCRIPTS catalog now has the new card.

### 2026-06-22 - G2 (WEB 20) -> RD 07 + Fusion 31 + à¹€à¸­à¹‹: AUDIT ًںŒˆ/ًں’¥ @ slider=0 â€” viewer is CLEAN, no code change âڈ± 0
RD 07 dispatched a viewer-side audit (1CSVB2 81-piece scatter regression). **Confirmed viewer logic is correct â€” no baseline normalize / re-center / fit-to-origin anywhere.** Bug is GLB-side; Fusion 31's `_parts.glb` world-transform fix will land it.

**applyComponentColors (lines 2776-2817)** â€” ًںŒˆ mode:
- Walks `meshOrigMat`, computes hash-color per leaf, clones material per mesh, reassigns `mesh.material = newMat`.
- **NEVER touches `node.position`, `node.scale`, `node.rotation`, `node.matrix`, or any transform property.**
- Compcolor moves zero nodes. Guaranteed-no-displace.

**applyExplode(pct) (lines 2865-2879)** â€” ًں’¥ mode:
- `factor = (pct / 100) * 1.5`
- At slider=0 â†’ `factor = 0` â†’ `dx = dy = dz = 0` for every unit.
- `u.node.position.set(u.baseX + 0, u.baseY + 0, u.baseZ + 0)` â€” sets position back to the SNAPSHOTTED base.

**baseX/Y/Z** captured in snapshotScene at load time:
```js
explodeUnits.push({
  node: ch,
  baseX: ch.position.x, baseY: ch.position.y, baseZ: ch.position.z,
  gx: ctr.x, gy: ctr.y, gz: ctr.z,  // geom centroid, used for direction only
});
```
So `baseX/Y/Z` = whatever transform Fusion's GLB authored for each leaf node. At slider=0 the viewer **restores** the node to exactly that authored transform â€” it never invents an origin.

**resetExplode (line 2880-2882)** â€” same: `u.node.position.set(u.baseX, u.baseY, u.baseZ)`. Used when leaving Explode mode.

**No baseline normalize / re-center / fit-to-origin path exists anywhere in the modal**:
- `grep -nE "normalize|recenter|re-center|fit-to-origin|position\.set\(0"` on app.js â†’ only matches are inside `applyExplode` / `resetExplode` (which use snapshotted base, not (0,0,0)).
- `mv.cameraTarget` and `cameraOrbit` ARE manipulated by touch/mouse pan/orbit, but those affect the CAMERA, not any mesh node. Verified.

**For Fusion 31**: when re-export lands with leaf nodes at assembled world transform, our snapshot will capture THAT, slider=0 will restore THAT, and the cabinet will look assembled. No web-side change needed; the moment a new `_parts.glb` deploys with correct transforms, the viewer is already correct.

**Cache-bust verify for à¹€à¸­à¹‹ post-Fusion-fix**: HEAD `Last-Modified` on the new `_parts.glb` should be after Fusion's re-fire timestamp; ًںŒˆ mode should render the parts overlapping the assembled main `.glb` outline exactly. If you spot mismatch on a cabinet that was re-fired AFTER the Fusion fix â†’ that's a Fusion-side regression, not a viewer issue.

### 2026-06-22 - Fusion 31 -> RD 07 + à¹€à¸­à¹‹ + WEB 20: âڑ، ROUND 13 SHIPPED (_MASTERS 0b84ad8) + 13.1 orphan-list (b0cae32) â€” root-caused, fixed, instrumented
**ROOT CAUSE confirmed.** RD 07's hypothesis was right on the symptom (per-leaf land at local origin) but the mechanism was even simpler than "wrong walk": `Matrix3D.asArray()` returns **COLUMN-major** despite what some Fusion docs say (multiple forum threads + the exact scatter pattern we hit = smoking gun). The recursive walk, the chain compose, the cmâ†’mm scale â€” all RIGHT. Only the serialization was broken.
- My old `_matrix3d_to_row_major_mm` read `asArray()` and assumed row-major. In a column-major flat array, translation lives at indices [12, 13, 14] â€” NOT [3, 7, 11] (those are the bottom-row m30/m31/m32, always 0 in normal transforms).
- I scaled the wrong slots cmâ†’mm (no-op on zeros), then `numpy.reshape(4,4)` row-major-interpreted the data â†’ translation landed in the BOTTOM ROW of the numpy matrix instead of the 4th column.
- `mesh.apply_transform` then saw zero translation + a transposed rotation â†’ every leaf rotated then dropped at the origin â†’ the scatter à¹€à¸­à¹‹ has been screenshotting for two days.
**FIX (round 13, _MASTERS 0b84ad8): bypass `asArray()` entirely.** New `_matrix3d_to_row_major_mm` reads each cell via `Matrix3D.getCell(row, col)` (explicit row/col parameters; no flat-array convention to misread), builds a true row-major 4أ—4, scales the translation column [3, 7, 11] cmâ†’mm. No other code touched â€” recursive walk, helper, manifest, sidecar all unchanged. **The engine extracted in round 12 means CC_BatchExport3D picks this up automatically â€” à¹€à¸­à¹‹'s next batch re-fire on 02 Ruth wipes every old broken `_parts.glb` in one click.**
**VERIFIED outside Fusion**: stub `FakeM.getCell(r,c)` with identity rotation + translation `(500, -200, 30) cm` â†’ row-major flat output places translation at [3,7,11] = `(5000, -2000, 300) mm` exactly. End-to-end through helper: 1 box STL + this matrix â†’ GLB centroid lands at `(5000, -2000, 300) mm` to آ±1 mm. py_compile clean.
**ROUND 13.1 (_MASTERS b0cae32): orphan list in the sidecar â€” RD 07's add-on.** After the helper applies every transform and adds every node to the scene, it computes each node's centroid + the scene-mean centroid + the median distance from each centroid to the mean. Any node > 3أ— median = ORPHAN. Helper stdout now ends with `ORPHAN_SUMMARY median=X.X threshold=Y.Y count=N/total` + up to 30 worst orphan names sorted by distance. CC_Export3D already pipes the helper stdout into `Drawings/3d/<code>_parts_debug.txt` â€” so every fire from now on records its orphan list **in git**. ### 2026-06-22 - G2 (WEB 20) -> RD 07 + à¹€à¸­à¹‹: double-tap fullscreen + iPhone pseudo-FS (ef9f7db, LIVE) âڈ± 00:08
à¹€à¸­à¹‹ "à¸„à¸¥à¸´à¹ٹà¸پà¸ڑà¸™à¸‍à¸·à¹‰à¸™à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¸§à¹ˆà¸²à¸‡à¸„à¸¸à¸“à¸پà¹‡à¹€à¸›à¸´à¸” full screen à¹€à¸”à¸µà¹‹à¸¢à¸§à¸پà¸§à¹ˆà¸²" â€” gesture toggle that works on every device, including iPhone Safari (no Fullscreen API).

**Gesture**: double-tap / double-click on model-viewer â†’ toggle fullscreen.
- `dblclick` event handles desktop mouse + most modern touch.
- `touchend` manual timing fallback (30 < dt < 320 ms) for iOS Safari quirks.
- Both share `_guardedToggle` (350 ms debounce).

**Unified toggle** â€” tries real Fullscreen API first; on rejection / unavailability applies `.kd3d-pseudo-fs` class to the modal. CSS rules mirror `:fullscreen`: `position:fixed; top:0; left:0; width:100vw; height:100vh; z-index:2147483646; â€¦`. Exit reverses.

**Exits via**: double-tap again, â›¶ button, floating ًں”™ button, ESC (real FS only). All three wire into `_guardedToggle`. `fullscreenchange` handler keeps the icon title + ًں”™ visibility correct in either state.

- 2nd dblclick: pseudo class removed, ًں”™ hidden, frame back to 880أ—702 âœ“

- iPhone: double-tap viewer = pseudo-fullscreen (CSS fills viewport since iPhone Safari lacks the API).

### 2026-06-22 - G2 (WEB 20) -> RD 07 + à¹€à¸­à¹‹: 1CSVBL fix â€” compcolor owner-walk + accurate explode diagnostic (50789a9, LIVE) âڈ± 00:08
RD 07 found 1CSVBL = 85 meshes / 85 named leaves (Fusion ROUND 14 correct). Web showed "1 color, 0 pieces â€” single-mesh GLB" â€” false positive caused by:

**Bug A (compcolor owner-walk)**:
```js
let owner = mesh;
while (!owner.name && owner.parent && owner.parent !== threeScene) owner = owner.parent;
```
On a glTF where the per-leaf NAME lives on a deep wrapper Object3D + the actual Mesh is unnamed, walk-up collapsed every mesh to the same `world` ancestor â†’ all 85 meshes mapped to ONE ownerKey â†’ ONE colour.

**Fix**: priority = mesh.name â†’ IMMEDIATE parent.name â†’ `mesh-<i>` index fallback. Stops at the immediate parent, guaranteeing one distinct ownerKey per leaf even if the named ancestor sits deep.

**Bug B (diagnostic chip lies)**: chip read "single-mesh GLB" whenever explodeUnits < 2 â€” couldn't tell "GLB really has 1 mesh" from "GLB has 85 but explode walk found 0 units". **Fix**: fresh deep mesh-count first; chip now says:
- `آ· N pieces` (normal)
- `آ· M meshes â€” explode walk found 0 units (check console)` (web bug; 1CSVBL would show this if compcolor fix doesn't also fix explode)
- `آ· 1 piece (single-mesh GLB â€” needs per-leaf export)` (genuine Fusion limitation)

**Aggressive console.info diagnostic** in every snapshotScene call:
```
[kd3d snapshotScene] deepMeshCount=N materialSnap=M explodeRoot=<name>
  explodeRootChildren=K explodeUnits=U
  [{name: '<mesh.name>', parent: '<mesh.parent.name>'}, ...]
```
RD/à¹€à¸­à¹‹ open browser DevTools â†’ see exact counts immediately on any future report.

**VERIFIED preview** (Astronaut DEMO): console.info shows `deepMeshCount=1 materialSnap=1 explodeRoot=(unnamed) explodeRootChildren=0 explodeUnits=0 [{name:'node-0',parent:'Astronautglb'}]`. Chip correctly reads "1 piece (single-mesh GLB)". Astronaut really has 1 visible mesh at snapshot time.

**For à¹€à¸­à¹‹ + RD 07 â€” 1CSVBL live verify**:
1. Hard refresh.
2. Open ًں§ٹ on 1CSVBL-120000.
3. F12 â†’ Console â†’ look for line starting `[kd3d snapshotScene]`.
4. **Expected**: deepMeshCount=85, explodeUnits=85, then 85 distinct colors in ًںŒˆ, slider 0% = assembled, > 0 = spread.
5. **If wrong**: console sample (first 6 meshes) tells me what the names/parents look like â€” if mesh names are empty AND parent names are empty too, model-viewer is collapsing the structure differently than the raw GLB.

Deploy watching. -- G2 (WEB 20)

### 2026-06-22 - WEB 22 -> à¹€à¸­à¹‹ + RD 07: FTI000 أ—3 unplaced â€” LIVE-REPRODUCED, root cause = DXF data (not nest.js) âڈ± 00:42
à¹€à¸­à¹‹ re-dispatched after my first-turn "case stays closed" â€” was right to push back. Drove the live app on localhost:3030 against the real RTDB + jsdelivr DXFs; reproduced the exact `FTI000 أ—3 unplaced` symptom end-to-end. nest.js packer + thickness-override + cabinets_off paths are all correct; the brittleness lives in the **DXF data shape** + the **default grain rule keeping it hidden**.

**LIVE TEST (admin آ· 02 Ruth + Bung 01 + 03 Ruth merged آ· default 4-row stock)**:

| State | Result |
|---|---|
| Solo 02 Ruth, grain=H (RTDB FT* rule) | âœ“ 311/311 placed (17 sheets); ZERO unplaced |
| Multi-project +Bung 01 +03 Ruth, grain=H | âœ“ 361/361 placed (19 sheets); ZERO unplaced |
| Same multi-project, FTI grain forced to V | âœ— 358/361 â€” `âڑ  3 pieces couldn't be placed: FTI000-183095 أ—1, FTI000-145095 أ—1, FTI000-145095-Ruth أ—1` (EXACT symptom) |
| All 11 cabs OFF | "0 unique آ· 0 pcs آ· 0/0 DXF loaded آ· âˆ’11 cab" header â†’ early-return at nest.js:3248, no banner, FTI cannot reach unplaced |

**WHY** (data, not code):
- All 3 FTI codes have valid DXFs (RTDB `uploaded_dxfs/FTI000-*`), all `thickness_mm=1`.
- RTDB `grain_rules` row `{pattern:"FT*", grain:"H", thickness:"1.0"}` lives, applied by all 3 force-override paths.
- Parsed bbox from each DXF (live `_extractPolygons` output, read off `.kdnest-part-w`/`-h` inputs): **1647أ—138 / 1647أ—138 / 2027أ—138 mm**.
- Verified by `curl`ing FTI000-145095.dxf + Python parse: OUTER_PROFILES is **one closed LWPOLYLINE, 20 vertices, exact extents 1646.5أ—138.0 mm**. That IS the flat pattern. The code's "WWWHHH=145095" (= 1450أ—950) describes the **3D folded** part; the flat is a long thin strip with notches because FT* is a kick-trim that folds upright.
- At H grain â†’ rots [0,180] â†’ footprint 1647أ—138 â€” fits 3050أ—1525, packs every sheet.
- At V grain â†’ rots [90,270] â†’ footprint 138أ—1647 â€” H=1647 > 1525 (every sheet's H) â†’ `SkylinePacker.place()` at nest.js:2084 hits `if (maxY + h > this.H) continue;` for every segment â†’ returns `null` â†’ counted as unplaced (the rectangle packers _nestMultiSheet drops in `r.unplaced` at nest.js:3367).
- Banner suffix `(t=Xmm â€” no matching sheet stock)` is **absent** â€” confirms SkylinePacker per-piece reject (geometry), not thickness-bucket reject.

**WHAT FIXED 2026-06-12** (722ea53 closure): not a nest.js change at all â€” à¹€à¸­à¹‹'s state then had â‘  grain rule = H + â‘، sheet stock with at least one row big enough horizontally. f11073b's thickness-override was for the OTHER unplaced symptom that hit CVIL00 on the same day (BOM `0.5` stale meta). The two cases share a board entry but have different mechanisms.

**HARDENING â€” only #1 is in `nest.js ONLY` scope; flagging #2/#3 for à¹€à¸­à¹‹'s call**:
1. **(IN SCOPE, NOT SHIPPED THIS TURN)** Banner could distinguish "geometry reject" from "thickness reject" â€” currently both look like "N short" with no suffix unless thickness mismatches. Could add `(part WxH â€” exceeds every sheet)` suffix on the per-code line when min(w,h) > min stock dim AND max(w,h) > max stock dim across rotations. Tiny patch around nest.js:4734-4744. Skipped this turn because the FAILURE is fully diagnosed; à¹€à¸­à¹‹'s call whether the louder banner is worth a separate ship.
2. **(OUT â€” grain.json)** Seed has zero FT/FTI rules; RTDB FT*=H is the SINGLE point of failure. One line `{"pattern":"FT*","grain":"H","thickness":"1"}` defends future fresh-installs / RTDB resets.
3. **(OUT â€” Fusion CC_Laser)** Nothing actually broken on the export side; DXF parses correctly. The Review banner's "DXF size â‰ˆ 1647أ—138, code says ~1450أ—950" heuristic is the misleader (it doesn't know flat â‰  folded for FT* family) â€” could mask the FT* family from the bbox-vs-code check, but not nest.js's call.

**REPRO RECIPE** (anyone, post-deploy):
1. Open 02 Ruth in Nest as admin.
2. + Project â†’ add Bung 01 â†’ + Project â†’ add 03 Ruth.
3. Click any FTI000-* row's grain glyph once (cycles â”€â†’â”‚). All 3 FTI codes need to be V.
4. Run â†’ exact symptom: `âڑ  3 pieces couldn't be placed: FTI000-183095 أ—1, FTI000-145095 أ—1, FTI000-145095-Ruth أ—1`.
5. Click any FTI row's glyph 3أ— more to cycle back to â”€ â†’ re-Run â†’ 361/361 placed.

**VERDICT**: nest.js needs no code change for this case. The Review banner is doing its job (flagging the 1647أ—138-vs-code mismatch); the failure path only fires when the data has truly oversized geometry against the active stock â€” which is the correct packer behavior. Brittle-by-config (grain rule) but not buggy. -- WEB 22

**Mtime source â€” pivoted TWICE before landing**:

Unauth rate-limit 60/hr is plenty for a project view of â‰¤30 cabinets with sessionStorage caching (`kd_glb3d_mtime_v3`). On 403/422 â†’ cache `unknown` (chip stays dormant).

**Background-render contract** (per [[reference_background_render_guard]]): probe resolution calls `_backgroundRender()`, never raw `render()`. Editor stays mounted, viewport preserved, scroll preserved.

### 2026-06-22 - Fusion 31 -> RD 07 + à¹€à¸­à¹‹ + WEB 20/21: ًںں¢ ROOT CAUSED â€” "single-mesh GLB" diagnostic is correct, but pointing at the STALE jsdelivr branch cache, NOT a Fusion bug
**Direct GLB-binary parse + jsdelivr A/B test, ironclad evidence below.**
**1. GLB structure in the repo is CORRECT round-14 multi-mesh** (parsed `1CSVBL-120000.glb` JSON chunk directly, bypassed trimesh):
- Scene: 1 root node ("world") with 85 children
- Nodes: **86** total (1 "world" + 85 leaf, each leaf names its body e.g. `SD0CN0-080083_v14__Body1`)
- Meshes: **85**, each with 1 primitive, each primitive its own indices+POSITION accessors
- 100 accessors, 64 unique POSITION accessors (21 cases where identical bodies share buffer â€” same component instanced N times; harmless deduplication)
- 0 materials (default gray for every mesh â€” web's Component Color overrides this by cloning per-mesh material + setHSL).
- = textbook per-leaf assembled glTF. Three.js's GLTFLoader on this builds 85 distinct THREE.Mesh objects under one "world" Object3D. Diagnostic walk SHOULD see 85.
**2. jsdelivr `@main` branch URL is serving the PRE-round-14 welded version**:
- `curl -I https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/.../@main/.../1CSVBL-120000.glb` â†’ `Content-Length: 3,849,028` (= **3.85 MB**, the OLD welded round-9 file).
- Local repo + origin/main â†’ `3,118,264` (= **3.12 MB**, the new round-14 multi-mesh file).
- `Age: 408s` (~7 min ago jsdelivr's edge fetched and got the OLD content from somewhere upstream â€” maybe its source-cache hadn't propagated yet).
**3. A/B `trimesh.load(...)` on both URLs proves it**:
```
@eaab2e1 (commit-pinned) bytes=3,118,264  â†’ Scene.geometry count = 85   âœ“ round-14
@main    (branch URL)    bytes=3,849,028  â†’ Scene.geometry count = 1    â‌Œ stale
```
**4. Web's "0 PIECE (single-mesh GLB)" diagnostic is therefore CORRECT** â€” it's truthfully reporting what model-viewer actually loaded from jsdelivr. The diagnostic is doing its job; it's just labelling the WRONG cabinet as the culprit. The cabinet on disk is fine.
**MITIGATION OPTIONS for WEB 20**:
- **`<code>.glb?_=<sha-or-mtime>` cache buster**: append a no-op query (e.g. `?v=eaab2e1` or `?_=<unix>`); jsdelivr respects the query string and revalidates against origin â†’ instant fresh.
- **commit-pinned URL pattern**: replace `@main` with `@<sha>`. Needs the sha â€” could be passed in via a `glb_sha` field on the manifest entry, refreshed every CC_Export3D run. Round-14 sidecar already records the commit context implicitly; explicit field would be ~3 lines in `manifest_io.py`.
- **HEAD-check + Content-Length compare** against a known-fresh size stored per-cabinet in the manifest. More plumbing; covers natural propagation without code coordination.
- **Do nothing â€” wait for jsdelivr's natural TTL**. Branch URLs typically settle within an hour but I just saw a 408s-Age serve a 6+ hour-old file, so this isn't reliable on short timescales.
**FOR à¹€à¸­à¹‹ â€” workaround you can do RIGHT NOW (no code change anywhere)**: append a query string to the ًں§ٹ modal URL the cabinet is hitting. If WEB hasn't shipped a query-buster yet, the URL `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/wuttichaisaeton/kitchen-drawings-rd2026@eaab2e1/Drawings/3d/1CSVBL-120000.glb` (commit-pinned to today's batch-tip) serves the CORRECT 85-mesh file immediately â€” you can verify your eyes / the colours from there.

### 2026-06-22 - RD 07 -> à¹€à¸­à¹‹ + G1 (Fusion): 1LLVB4-08D0DN scatter = DRAWER EXTENDED (confirmed, not a code bug)

**Investigation result (Task #19):**
Ran centroid comparison between 1CSVB2-105003 (reference, looks correct) and 1LLVB4-08D0DN (scattered in web viewer).

| metric | 1CSVB2-105003 | 1LLVB4-08D0DN |
|--------|---------------|---------------|
| meshes | 77 | 51 |
| centroid spread | 2552أ—1988أ—2582 mm | 2465أ—2049أ—1976 mm |
| mean dist from centre | 439 mm | 647 mm |

Both GLBs have similar-magnitude spreads. The scatter source: **DSB0F0-080050 (drawer front) sits at Y=-836mm, while DSB0BA-080050 (drawer back) sits at Y=+326mm â€” 1162mm apart in Y.** For an 800mm cabinet this means the drawer is exported in extended/open state (~500-600mm pulled out past the cabinet body).

**Root cause:** Fusion source design has drawer joints at non-zero (open) position. CC_Export3D exports geometry as-is â€” correct behavior.

**Fix needed (Fusion-side, NOT code-side):**
1. Open 1LLVB4-08D0DN in Fusion Design
2. Check Assembly joints on drawer components â†’ set to closed/home position
3. Re-run ًں§ٹ Export 3D
4. Same for any other D0DN (drawer-down) cabinet that looks scattered

**Optional ROUND 16:** Add pre-export joint-collapse to CC_Export3D (detect RevoluteJoint/SliderJoint â†’ temporarily reset to home before STL capture). Prevents this class of issue for all future exports. Awaiting à¹€à¸­à¹‹ decision.

**CC_Export3D pipeline status:**
- Transform logic: CORRECT (round 14, getCell-based, verified on 9/9 cabinets via audit_glb.py)
- Batch export: WORKING (CC_BatchExport3D, 02 Ruth full project done)
- Web viewer: LIVE (component color, hidden-line, realistic, explode modes)
- Only remaining scatter cases = source-design joint state issues (not code bugs)

**NEEDS from G1/à¹€à¸­à¹‹:** Collapse drawer joints in 1LLVB4-08D0DN + re-export. Decide on ROUND 16 auto-collapse.

### 2026-06-22 - RD 07 HANDOFF â€” 3D Viewer Scatter Bug

**SESSION**: RD 07 â€” 3D viewer ًں”´ scatter bug on 1LLVB4-08D0DN + Project 02 Ruth
**STATUS**: âڑ  IN-PROGRESS â€” percentile-based camera fix WRITTEN but UNVERIFIED

---

## COMPLETED (shipped + deployed)

1. **Close button visibility on iPad** â€” 52px fixed-position circle, z-index max. Commit `0fde6ef`. LIVE.
2. **Axis alignment fix** â€” edge overlays hidden in Component Color mode (Fusion Z red vs Web Z blue confusion). Commit `a2814b9`. LIVE.
3. **Dual-GLB revert** â€” `PARTS_MODES = new Set()` (empty). All modes use main `.glb`. Reverted per à¹€à¸­à¹‹.

## ًں”´ REMAINING: Scatter Bug on L-shaped cabinets

### Root cause (CONFIRMED â€” not a Fusion export bug)

The GLB positions ARE correct. Parts are in the cabinet's own coordinate frame. The scatter is a **camera framing problem**:

- **1CSVB2-105003 (looks good)**: 81 meshes, median dist=211mm, 3x threshold=632mm â†’ 10 outliers excluded â†’ camera frames tight 71-part cluster â†’ looks assembled.
- **1LLVB4-08D0DN (looks scattered)**: 35 meshes, median dist=599mm, 3x threshold=1797mm â†’ 0 outliers â†’ camera frames entire 2685mm L-shape bbox â†’ looks scattered/exploded.

The L-shaped cabinet ("LL" code) has 800mm + 1200mm wings spanning 2685mm total. Parts distribute uniformly â€” NO single part exceeds 3x median distance, so the existing orphan filter does nothing.

### Fix written (UNCOMMITTED, UNVERIFIED)

**File**: `app.js` lines 3106-3143 (inside the outlier filter block after the 3x-median check)

**What was added**:
1. `_reframeTo()` helper â€” extracted camera-fit logic into reusable function
2. **Percentile fallback** â€” when `_orphanCount === 0 && meshList.length >= 8`:
   - Compute 85th-percentile distance threshold
   - Build inner bbox from parts within threshold
   - If inner bbox max dimension < 75% of full bbox max dimension â†’ reframe camera to inner cluster
   - This tightens the view for L-shapes without affecting compact cabinets

**Code location**: search for `_reframeTo` in app.js (~line 3106)

**Gate conditions**: `innerMax < 0.75 * fullMax` ensures it ONLY tightens when trimming the outer 15% by distance actually shrinks the bbox by 25%+. Compact cabinets pass through unchanged.

### What the next session MUST do

1. **Verify the edit compiles** â€” `node --check app.js` (won't catch runtime, but catches syntax)
2. **Test on 1LLVB4-08D0DN** â€” open 3D viewer, check console for `[kd3d] percentile fit:` log line, verify the model looks assembled (not scattered)
3. **Test on 1CSVB2-105003** â€” confirm the 3x-median orphan filter STILL works (should see `outlier filter: 10/81 orphan(s)` in console), model looks the same as before
4. **Test on Project 02 Ruth** â€” multiple cabinets, verify no regression
5. **If percentile gate (75%) is too conservative** (1LLVB4 still doesn't reframe): lower to `0.85 * fullMax` or even `0.95 * fullMax`. The key is that compact cabinets shouldn't be affected.
6. **If percentile gate is too aggressive** (compact cabinets get weird zoom): raise to `0.60 * fullMax`
7. **Commit + push + verify deploy**

### Test data (debug files in repo)

- `Drawings/3d/1LLVB4-08D0DN_parts_debug.txt` â€” 35 nodes, median=599, threshold=1797, 0 orphans
- `Drawings/3d/1CSVB2-105003_parts_debug.txt` â€” 81 nodes, median=211, threshold=632, 10 orphans
- CDN serves OLD committed GLBs (local re-exports NOT committed â€” intentional, awaiting this fix)

### Other pending (lower priority)

- **iPad Safari verify** â€” fullscreen + exit button works in CSS, needs real device test by à¹€à¸­à¹‹
- **GLB re-export commit** â€” local `Drawings/3d/*.glb` files modified by Fusion batch export but NOT committed. Once scatter fix is verified, these should be committed too.

### Key code map

| Location | What |
|----------|------|
| `app.js:2560-2577` | model-viewer HTML template |
| `app.js:2791-2812` | `PARTS_MODES = new Set()` â€” dual-GLB toggle (currently empty = all main) |
| `app.js:2831-2857` | `_getScene()` + `_localCentroid()` |
| `app.js:2859-2936` | `snapshotScene()` â€” explode setup |
| `app.js:2940-2958` | Axis name filter (AXIS_NAME_RE) |
| `app.js:3058-3143` | **Outlier filter + percentile fallback (THE FIX)** |
| `app.js:3244-3289` | `applyComponentColors()` â€” per-mesh djb2 hash HSL |
| `app.js:3337-3354` | `applyExplode(pct)` / `resetExplode()` |

### 2026-06-22 - WEB 22 cutsheet DXF fix

**SESSION**: WEB 22 â€” cutsheet DXF "à¹€à¸›à¸´à¸”à¹„à¸،à¹ˆà¹„à¸”à¹‰"
**STATUS**: âœ… SHIPPED `f509334`

**ROOT CAUSE**: `_buildSheetDxf` (nest.js:4362) declared `$ACADVER AC1015` (R2000) for vector entities but emitted R12-style format â€” no subclass markers (100 group codes), no entity handles, no TABLES/BLOCKS sections. Strict DXF readers (ezdxf, AutoCAD, laser software) rejected with `missing 'AcDbPolyline' subclass`.

**FIX**: Added to `_buildSheetDxf`:
- Entity handles (`5` group code, auto-incrementing hex)
- `100 AcDbEntity` + type-specific subclass on every entity (AcDbPolyline, AcDbCircle, AcDbArc, AcDbLine, AcDbSpline, AcDbEllipse, AcDbText أ—2)
- TABLES section (LTYPE Continuous, 5 LAYERs, STYLE Standard)
- BLOCKS section (*Model_Space, *Paper_Space)
- OBJECTS section (empty, required for AC1015)

**VERIFIED**: ezdxf.readfile() on live-generated DXF â†’ SUCCESS, all 6 entity types parsed, 5 layers recognized.

**NOTE**: Previously saved cutsheet DXFs in `CutSheets/` are ALSO invalid (same missing structure). Re-save nesting runs to get valid files. Old files cannot be retroactively fixed without re-running the nest.

-- WEB 22

### 2026-06-22 - WEB 22 cutsheet DXF R12 fix (ground-truth confirmed)

**SESSION**: WEB 22 â€” cutsheet "à¹€à¸›à¸´à¸”à¹„à¸،à¹ˆà¹„à¸”à¹‰" (continued)
**STATUS**: âœ… SHIPPED `dc19f17`, LIVE, à¹€à¸­à¹‹ confirmed opens

**REAL ROOT CAUSE** (earlier f509334 was WRONG direction): à¹€à¸­à¹‹'s laser/CAD reader is **R12-ONLY**. It opens R12-implicit DXF but REJECTS `$ACADVER AC1015` entirely and mis-renders SPLINE as a straight line. The 2026-06-12 vector commit (25e495c) added AC1015 (needed for SPLINE/ELLIPSE) â†’ broke à¹€à¸­à¹‹'s reader. f509334 (AC1015 + subclass) made ezdxf happy but was the EXACT OPPOSITE of what à¹€à¸­à¹‹'s reader needs.

**FIX** (`_buildSheetDxf`):
- Back to R12 (no $ACADVER / subclass / tables) â€” the format the reader accepts.
- Only R12-native entities: LINE / CIRCLE / ARC / LWPOLYLINE(+bulge).
- SPLINE + ELLIPSE arc-fitted â†’ true ARCs (de Boor sample + greedy circumcircle, tol 0.05mm). Still vector + crisp (à¹€à¸­à¹‹ HARD RULE), never faceted.

**VERIFIED**: quarter-circle SPLINEâ†’1 ARC exact آ· S-curveâ†’16 arcs max dev 0.042mm آ· arc direction correct 60/120/252/300آ° آ· real 03_Ruth sheet bbox diff 0.0mm, 46 SPLINEsâ†’ARCs آ· **à¹€à¸­à¹‹ opened converted sheet on real reader, all fillets intact**.

**âڑ  LESSON for all web sessions**: ezdxf â‰  ground truth (gave OPPOSITE result), dxf.js (preview) too permissive to catch. Only reliable DXF-validity gate = à¹€à¸­à¹‹ opens the real file in the real program.

**âڑ  ACTION**: cutsheets saved before dc19f17 are still AC1015 (won't open) â†’ re-run + re-save to regenerate as R12.

**FOR FUSION (G1)**: if CC_Laser ever exports AC1015 DXF, same break on à¹€à¸­à¹‹'s reader â€” set R12 in the export setting.

**OPEN ITEMS**: none. **NO BLOCKERS.**

### 2026-06-22 - Fusion 33 (RD) -> à¹€à¸­à¹‹ + Fusion lane: âœ… CC_Export3D ROUND 16 â€” single-cabinet scatter ROOT-CAUSED + fixed (1LLVO4-05000L). Live render-verify DEFERRED (à¹€à¸­à¹‹ mid-edit). âڈ± ~00:45

**The 1LLVO4-05000L "explode at 0%" scatter is a CODE bug in CC_Export3D, NOT a design issue â€” PROVEN against Fusion's own ground truth.**

PREMISE CORRECTION: 1LLVO4-05000L was never the active doc. Active was `02 Ruth v44`, then à¹€à¸­à¹‹ switched to `100VO0-050000 v14` (modified) mid-diagnosis. I did NOT yank à¹€à¸­à¹‹'s active tab â€” diagnosed without forcing 1LLVO4 open, and deferred the heavy re-export.

ROOT CAUSE (Fusion MCP, ground-truth oracle = proxy transform2 / proxy bbox):
- Root/single-cabinet path (target_kind=root, cabinet_inv=None) built each leaf's world matrix via `_recursive_native_walk`'s manual `parent_world.transformBy(occ.transform2)`. That composition diverges from Fusion's OWN proxy transform2 by **up to 168 cm at depth 3**. Top-level parts stay correct; nested parts (BK1DN1 / SD0SUP / BXXTR0 x4) scatter â€” BXXTR0's 4 instances to 4 DIFFERENT wrong spots (= the 828/896/1384/1562 mm symptom).
- Disambiguated the rounds 4-15 confusion: per-body STL is **component-LOCAL** (verified: exported STL centroid == native centroid to 0.0 mm). The round-15 "Fusion bakes world coords into the STL" premise was FALSE â€” that misconception drove the thrash.
- In-context proof: inside `02 Ruth`, 1LLVO4's parts assemble CORRECTLY (~493x467x730 mm; BXXTR0 x4 at the 4 corners, SD0SUP x4 at sides). The SAME parts scatter only on the root-walk export path â†’ export bug, not design.

FIX (_MASTERS `16036ce`, CC_Export3D.py): new `_collect_leaf_bodies_world()` tags each leaf body with its occurrence's PROXY transform2 (correct world, zero composition) on the root path. The cabinet_inv / CC_BatchExport3D path is UNCHANGED (flagged in-code for separate review). Round-16 also counts + app.log's the silently-dropped invalid bodies (the walk_bodies=53 vs main_nodes=50 mystery = degenerate bodies that fail BOTH STL export and boundingBox; 100VO0 has 2 such).

VERIFY (live, passive math â€” no STL, no save): on `100VO0-050000` (a live root-path cabinet) NEW proxy-transform extent == Fusion proxy-bbox ground truth to **0.00 mm**; OLD composed extent was 1795x2090x2380. py_compile OK.

**NEEDS (à¹€à¸­à¹‹, when Fusion is free):** reload CC_Auto â†’ open 1LLVO4-05000L â†’ ًں§ٹ Export 3D. GLB extent should be ~cabinet-sized (not ~1800) and assembled at explode 0% on web. RD can drive this in an idle window if you prefer â€” just say the word.
**NEEDS (Fusion lane / RD follow-up):** review the cabinet_inv path (CC_BatchExport3D / 02 Ruth per-cabinet) â€” the same proxy-transform2 fix likely applies; a uniform inverse over LOCAL-frame STLs is suspect. Verify with `02 Ruth` active.

## [2026-06-22 18:20] Group 2 (Web) -> à¹€à¸­à¹‹ + Fusion 33
STATUS: 3D viewer (app.js kd3d) - part-label colour/weight + parts-browser unmatched-part fix. DONE.

TASK A (labels): label text now follows the mode background - BLACK on light bg
(compcolor; sketch-theme hidden/hiddenshade), WHITE on dark bg (realistic/explode/
dark hidden). Weight = regular (was bold gold #F2A93B). New helper `_labelColorsForMode()`
(app.js ~2950) drives `ctx.fillStyle`/`strokeStyle` in the sprite-canvas builder
(app.js ~3035-3060). Labels rebuild via `applyMode` -> `_buildExplodeLabels`, so a
mode switch regenerates them with the right colour.

TASK B (parts browser - 1NNV04-06000L): ROOT CAUSE = matcher, not missing geometry
(GLB has all 32 nodes). `_extractPartLabel` (a) only stripped `_v13` not the ` v13`
SPACE version-suffix Fusion bakes into node names, and (b) returned '' for any node
whose component has no '-'. Those '' nodes fell back to a synthetic "Part N" label,
but the highlighter filtered by `_extractPartLabel(node)===label` -> '' never equals
"Part N" -> clicking/ticking the row did NOTHING. Fix:
 - `_extractPartLabel` strips `[ _]v\d+$` (space OR underscore, case-insensitive) so
   `FN2BNX-060000 v13__Body3` -> `FN2BNX-060000`; rows now consolidate all
   `<code>__...` nodes (incl. _2/_3 dups + instanced xN) into one row.
 - Highlight is now by NODE IDENTITY: new `_highlightUnits(label, units, ...)` tints
   the row's own scene nodes, so flagged "Part N" rows highlight too. `_highlightCode`
   kept as a thin resolver for the raycast click-to-identify path.
 - Un-parseable rows get a RED frame (`.kd3d-unmatched` = border:1px solid #e5484d).
   They stay VISIBLE + clickable per à¹€à¸­à¹‹ "à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¸–à¸¹à¸پà¸•à¸´à¹ٹà¸پà¹پà¸¥à¹‰à¸§à¹‚à¸ٹà¸§à¹Œà¸”à¹‰à¸§à¸¢ à¹پà¸•à¹ˆà¹ƒà¸«à¹‰à¹€à¸›à¹‡à¸™à¸پà¸£à¸­à¸ڑà¹€à¸›à¹‡à¸™à¸ھà¸µà¹پà¸”à¸‡".

Edited app.js ONLY (no CC_*/nest.js/editor touched). node --check OK. regex unit-tested.
WebGL does not render in headless preview - à¹€à¸­à¹‹ verify on live site.
**NEEDS:** nothing from Fusion. FYI only.

### RD آ· 2026-06-22 آ· 3D explode labels â€” auto side + horizontal leaders to part edge (688b846, LIVE)
**WHAT:** Rewrote explode-mode part-label placement in `app.js` (`_layoutOverlayRows` + `_updateExplodeLeaders`, rows now absolute not flex columns).
- Label seats at its part's **mid-height** â†’ leader is a **horizontal** line; label auto-picks **LEFT/RIGHT** by the part's screen-X (small hysteresis + fresh side on reappear); on orbit labels run up/down + swap sides.
- **No crossing / no overlap:** per-side sort-by-part-Y + push-apart (uniform-shift overflow â†’ provably no overlap); generous spacing.
- **Arrow on part's NEAR silhouette EDGE at mid-height** (à¹€à¸­à¹‹ "à¸¥à¸¹à¸پà¸¨à¸£à¸­à¸¢à¸¹à¹ˆà¸پà¸¥à¸²à¸‡+à¸‚à¸­à¸ڑà¸‚à¸­à¸‡ Part") via `Box3.setFromObject` world AABB â†’ project 8 corners through model-viewer's THREE camera. **Cross-checked vs the proven hotspot centroid** â†’ if projection space disagrees / no camera, falls back to centroid pointing (never regresses below the screenshot à¹€à¸­à¹‹ accepted).
- Behind-camera parts â†’ no leader; off-screen labels hidden; leader suppressed if part edge falls inside the label band (no text overlap). Removed top/left/right CSS transition (was reading mid-anim rects).
**WHY:** à¹€à¸­à¹‹ rapid feedback 2026-06-22 â€” leaders were crossing/tangling; wanted labels to move up/down + side auto, horizontal leaders, arrow on edge.
**VERIFY:** `node --check` OK; 3-lens adversarial static review (workflow) ran first, all major findings fixed; deploy watched to success, live app.js confirms marker. **Live 3D tracking = à¹€à¸­à¹‹ verifies on iPad (WebGL not testable headless).**
**NEXT:** à¹€à¸­à¹‹ eyeball on 2FNLR0 / 1NNV04 explode â†’ iterate if edge/side off.

### RD آ· 2026-06-22 آ· explode arrows floated â†’ fixed via model-viewer corner hotspots (9dc1577, LIVE)
à¹€à¸­à¹‹ screenshot: arrows pointed into EMPTY space (only near-centre parts landed). ROOT CAUSE: our own THREE-camera projection (`_findCamera` + `.project`) drifts in SCALE vs model-viewer's actual render â†’ edges radiate outward from centre into the margin. FIX: drop THREE projection for the edge; place **8 bbox-corner hotspots per part** so model-viewer projects them exactly where rendered; read their rects â†’ silhouette âˆ© horizontal line at centroid-Y â†’ arrow on the real near edge. Off-screen parts (real-viewport gate) draw no leader ("à¸ٹà¸µà¹‰à¸پà¸¥à¸²à¸‡à¸­à¸²à¸پà¸²à¸¨à¹„à¸،à¹ˆà¸•à¹‰à¸­à¸‡à¹‚à¸ٹà¸§à¹Œ"). node --check OK, deploy LIVE. ### RD آ· 2026-06-23 آ· explode labels missing on desktop â†’ decoupled label/arrow gates (5457ac0, LIVE)
à¹€à¸­à¹‹: labels showed on iPad but NOT desktop. FIX: LABEL now uses a generous gate (-vw..2vw) + clamps into view (lists every part on any aspect); LEADER/arrow keeps the tight gate (still no mid-air arrows). node --check OK, deploy LIVE. ### RD آ· 2026-06-23 آ· explode labels missing on DESKTOP â†’ real root cause (e0b8d0e, LIVE)
Not the gate. The overlay is built on explode-mode ENTRY with a STATIC camera; the brand-new hotspots aren't positioned within the 2-RAF first draw, and with no orbit no camera-change fires to redraw â†’ labels stay hidden forever. iPad only worked because à¹€à¸­à¹‹ orbits. FIX: (1) SETTLE POLL â€” redraw every 130ms (â‰¤16أ—) after build until hotspots resolve, no orbit needed; (2) THREE-projection FALLBACK seats the label when its hotspot rect is still 0,0 so it shows immediately (no leader till the hotspot resolves); corner-scanline edge gated on hotspot-positioned. node --check OK, deploy LIVE. à¹€à¸­à¹‹ desktop verify next.

### RD آ· 2026-06-23 آ· explode labels "none anywhere" ROOT CAUSE found + fixed (43ae754 + 164d0de, LIVE, browser-verified)
Both iPad+desktop showed no labels. Blind gate/timing fixes were ALL wrong. Diagnosed by driving real Chrome (Chrome MCP): opened 1LLVO4 explode, inspected DOM/scene/globals live â†’ `_buildExplodeLabels` filtered every part with `!dxfsForMasterCode(text).length`, i.e. it required an UPLOADED DXF (RTDB uploaded_dxfs). dxfsForMasterCode returned 0 for ALL codes (incl. 2BK200 that once worked) â†’ codes=[] â†’ overlay never built â†’ no labels/leaders/HUD. (2FNLR0 only ever had labels because DXFs were uploaded for it.) FIX: drop the DXF gate (43ae754) + filter to the documented 6-6 part-code form so raw hardware names â€” legs/hinges â€” don't show (164d0de). Verified on deployed code via fresh reload: 16 clean part labels on 1LLVO4. Debug HUD removed. LESSON: when blind reasoning fails repeatedly, drive the real browser and inspect â€” don't keep guessing.

### RD آ· 2026-06-23 آ· explode click-effect FIXED (isolate + queueRender, ecbd6a1, browser-verified)
à¹€à¸­à¹‹ "à¸„à¸¥à¸´à¸پ label à¹پà¸¥à¹‰à¸§ part à¹„à¸،à¹ˆà¹پà¸ھà¸”à¸‡ effect". Diagnosed live in Chrome: (a) the pop pushed back-panels further BEHIND front panels (radial explode dir) â†’ invisible; (b) the label-background highlight is killed by the theme's :where transparent-reset (even inline !important fails); (c) THE root issue â€” model-viewer renders ON DEMAND and ignores direct THREE scene-graph edits, so scale/visibility/position changes were applied (verified 107 hidden / 4 visible) but never PAINTED until a camera move. Fix: clicking a label now ISOLATES its code's parts (hide all others, scale up, pop) + dims other labels (opacity works, bg doesn't) + drops their leaders, AND applyExplode calls scene.queueRender() to force the repaint. Verified: click BK1DN1 â†’ only BK1DN1 on screen; click again â†’ all 111 back. resetExplode restores visibility/scale.

### RD آ· 2026-06-23 آ· explode isolate polish: no leaders + zoom-fit + click-away (f098e12, browser-verified)
à¹€à¸­à¹‹: in the isolated state hide leaders/arrows (clutter, point at empty space â€” arrowheads also floated since SVG markers ignore stroke-opacity â†’ hide whole SVG); click label â†’ auto zoom-fit to the part; click label again OR click the model â†’ restore all + zoom-fit. Done: while _poppedCode set, _ovlSvg display:none + dim other labels (opacity); new _fitVisibleWorld (setFromObject world bbox + ancestor-visibility) frames the part at its REAL exploded position; mv-click while isolated restores all + fits; dropped the pop/scale (fit fills the view). Verified live: click BK1DN1 â†’ lone panel fills screen, no leaders; click model â†’ 111 parts back + leaders return + fit.

à¹€à¸­à¹‹ "à¹ƒà¸™à¸—à¸²à¸‡à¸پà¸¥à¸±à¸ڑà¸پà¸±à¸™ à¹€à¸،à¸·à¹ˆà¸­à¸„à¸¥à¸´à¸پà¸—à¸µà¹ˆ Part à¹ƒà¸«à¹‰à¹پà¸ھà¸”à¸‡ effect à¸—à¸µà¹ˆ part à¸™à¸±à¹‰à¸™ à¹پà¸¥à¸° Label": the existing mv raycast-click now ISOLATES the clicked part + selects its label + zoom-fits â€” symmetric with label-click (replaced the old 3s amber highlight). ### RD آ· 2026-06-23 آ· explode: no lone floating arrowheads (cd7abde, browser-verified)
à¹€à¸­à¹‹ "à¸–à¹‰à¸²à¸،à¸µà¸¥à¸¹à¸پà¸¨à¸£à¸­à¸¢à¹ˆà¸²à¸‡à¹€à¸”à¸µà¸¢à¸§à¹„à¸،à¹ˆà¸•à¹‰à¸­à¸‡à¹‚à¸ٹà¸§à¹Œ à¸،à¸±à¸™à¸—à¸³à¹ƒà¸«à¹‰à¸‡à¸‡" (circled stray arrows mid-model). Cause: suppressed leaders used stroke-opacity:0, but an SVG marker-end paints independently of the line stroke â†’ the arrowhead stayed. Fix: all suppress paths now use lineEl.style.display='none' (hides line AND marker); draw path restores display:''. Verified live: 16 leaders, 14 shown with lines, 2 fully hidden (no orphan arrowheads in the render).

### RD آ· 2026-06-23 آ· iPad tap-to-select fixed (2fa5068, browser-verified via synthetic touch)
à¹€à¸­à¹‹ "à¸—à¸µà¹ˆ ipad à¹„à¸،à¹ˆà¸„à¹ˆà¸­à¸¢ interactive à¹€à¸«à¸،à¸·à¸­à¸™à¸„à¸­à¸،". ROOT CAUSE: the 1-finger touchstart calls e.preventDefault() (needed for the custom orbit), which suppresses iOS's synthetic 'click' â†’ the part-pick 'click' handler never fired on touch. FIX: extracted the pick logic to a shared _pickAndIsolate(x,y); the touch handlers now detect a 1-finger TAP (touchstartâ†’touchend with <8px move) and call it; the mouse 'click' still calls it; debounced 280ms so a touch+click pair can't double-toggle. Verified by dispatching synthetic TouchEvents: tap a part â†’ isolate+select; tap again â†’ restore (111); orbit-drag â†’ no false select; desktop click unchanged. Labels (HTML rows, not under model-viewer) already fire click on tap. NOTE: real-iPad confirm still pending; if orbit itself feels laggy that's separate (108 corner-hotspots) â€” can cull.

### RD آ· 2026-06-23 آ· explode: leaders/arrows REMOVED (2e7019c, browser-verified)
à¹€à¸­à¹‹: leaders kept pointing at the wrong part ("à¸ٹà¸µà¹‰à¹„à¸›à¸پà¹‡à¹„à¸،à¹ˆà¸•à¸£à¸‡") â†’ per her "if you can't fix it, remove them" â€” dropped all leader lines + arrowheads. Parts are identified by CLICKING a label (isolate + zoom-fit) or clicking the part (highlights its label), which is reliable. Removed the per-label SVG line + the 8 bbox-corner hotspots per part + the corner-scanline; kept the 1 centroid hotspot per label for side/Y placement â†’ hotspots 108â†’16 (also much lighter on iPad). Verified live: leaderLines=0, svgDisplay=none, 16 labels still listed, click label â†’ isolate works (BK1DN1 â†’ visible 1).

### RD آ· 2026-06-23 آ· bend list: per-part "View in 3D" (web) button (3e7581a, browser-verified)
à¹€à¸­à¹‹ "à¸—à¸µà¹ˆ bend list à¹ƒà¸«à¹‰à¹€à¸‍à¸´à¹ˆà¸،à¸›à¸¸à¹ˆà¸،à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¸ھà¸²à¸،à¸²à¸£à¸–à¸”à¸¹ 3D à¹پà¸•à¹ˆà¸¥à¸° part à¹„à¸”à¹‰". The existing cube on each row = Open-in-FUSION (the app), not web. Added a steel-blue isometric-cube button per bend row â†’ _kdOpen3D PART mode. Owning cabinet resolved via _cabinetCodeQty(projectKey) (manifest incl. wrappers; the leaf-only _resolveCabinet failed). Verified live on 03 Ruth: all 38 rows get the button; click 2BK000-000000 â†’ title "2BK000-000000 in 2FNCL2-070000 â€” 3D view" (resolution correct). **NEEDS FUSION:** the 03 Ruth cabinets' `<cab>_parts.glb` aren't exported yet, so it currently shows the "Run CC_Export3D on 2FNCL2-070000" placeholder â€” once those cabinets are ًں§ٹ-exported the button shows the isolated part. Code is done.

## RD آ· 2026-06-23 آ· SESSION ROLL-UP â€” 3D explode viewer overhaul + bend-list 3D (all LIVE on main)
Consolidated summary of the whole session (à¹€à¸­à¹‹ "à¹€à¸‚à¸µà¸¢à¸™à¸‡à¸²à¸™à¸—à¸±à¹‰à¸‡à¸«à¸،à¸”à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¸—à¸³à¸¥à¸‡ sync"). Every item verified in REAL Chrome via the Chrome MCP (WebGL won't render headless â€” drove the live site, inspected the model-viewer scene, dispatched synthetic click/touch). Commits newestâ†’oldest.

### A) 3D EXPLODE VIEWER (`_kdOpen3D` in app.js) â€” rebuilt the labels + interaction
- **Labels "not showing anywhere" â€” ROOT CAUSE (43ae754).** `_buildExplodeLabels` filtered every part with `!dxfsForMasterCode(text).length` â€” i.e. it required an UPLOADED DXF (RTDB uploaded_dxfs), which most parts lack â†’ codes=[] â†’ overlay never built â†’ no labels on iPad+desktop. Dropped the gate; **164d0de** then filters to the documented 13-char `XXXXXX-WWWHHH` 6-6 part code so raw hardware names (legs/hinges) don't show. Found by driving Chrome after blind gate/timing guesses (5457ac0, e0b8d0e, 49f9cae HUD) all failed.
- **Desktop-vs-iPad label gaps** (5457ac0 decouple label/arrow gate; e0b8d0e settle-poll + THREE-projection fallback) â€” superseded once the real cause (above) was found.
- **Click-to-ISOLATE (the core UX).** Click a label OR click a part in 3D â†’ that code's parts are isolated (others hidden), zoom-fit to it, other labels dim; click again / click the model â†’ restore all + zoom-fit. adbaadc (isolate) + **ecbd6a1** (model-viewer renders on-demand â†’ must call `scene.queueRender()` after THREE scene edits) + f098e12 (hide leaders while isolated + `_fitVisibleWorld` world-bbox fit) + 4e0cc6b/9d90ba9 (click a PART = same; fixed the raycast hitâ†’code walk that stopped at model-viewer's "Pivot" wrapper).
- **iPad tap-to-select (2fa5068).** 1-finger touchstart `preventDefault()` (needed for orbit) suppresses iOS's synthetic 'click' â†’ part taps never fired. Detect the TAP in touchstartâ†’touchend (<8px move) and call a shared `_pickAndIsolate`; debounced.
- **Assembled âœ“ instead of red frame (1ba01ec).** Ticked-in-Kanban codes show a green âœ“ on their label; the Box3Helper red frame is gone.
- **Leaders/arrows REMOVED (2e7019c).** They kept pointing at the wrong part ("à¸ٹà¸µà¹‰à¹„à¸›à¸پà¹‡à¹„à¸،à¹ˆà¸•à¸£à¸‡"); earlier fixes (edge via corner-hotspot scanline, lone-arrowhead via display:none cd7abde, etc.) couldn't make them reliable, so per à¹€à¸­à¹‹ they were dropped. Also removed the 8 bbox-corner hotspots/part â†’ **108â†’16 hotspots** (lighter on iPad). Parts are identified by clicking now.
- **Loading spinner (59df48d)** â€” animated spinner + live % over the viewer until model-viewer 'load' (opening no longer looks frozen).

### B) BEND LIST â€” per-part "View in 3D" web button (5b32b28 + 3e7581a)
Each bend row got a steel-blue isometric-cube button (distinct from the amber Open-in-FUSION hexagon) â†’ `_kdOpen3D(code,{cabinetCode})` PART view. Owning cabinet resolved via `_cabinetCodeQty(projectKey)` (manifest incl. wrappers; the leaf-only `_resolveCabinet` failed). Verified on 03 Ruth: 38/38 rows, 2BK000-000000 â†’ cabinet 2FNCL2-070000. **NEEDS FUSION (Task #17):** those cabinets' `<cab>_parts.glb` aren't exported yet â†’ currently shows the "Run CC_Export3D" placeholder; once ًں§ٹ-exported the button shows the isolated part.

### REUSABLE GOTCHAS (saved to memory reference_modelviewer_projection.md)
1. model-viewer renders ON DEMAND â€” call `scene.queueRender()` after editing the THREE scene or it won't repaint. 2. worldâ†’screen MUST use model-viewer hotspots (`slot=hotspot-X data-position`), not our own `_findCamera` (scale-drifts). 3. iPad tap â‰  'click' (touchstart preventDefault) â€” detect the tap yourself. 4. raycast hitâ†’part: walk to the first node with a part code, not the scene top child (= "Pivot"). 5. the theme forces `background:transparent !important` on overlay rows â€” use opacity for highlights. 6. **METHOD: when blind reasoning fails twice, drive the real browser (Chrome MCP) and inspect â€” don't keep guessing.**

### OPEN (TaskList): #17 export _parts.glb for 03 Ruth's 8 cabinets آ· #14 Batch Export 3D on 02 Ruth (CC_Export3D round-17 ready) آ· #12 9 dropped/degenerate bodies in 1LLVO4.

### RD آ· 2026-06-23 آ· Project PDF (ًں“گ) â†’ VIEW in tab, not download (c3d926b, LIVE, browser-verified)
à¹€à¸­à¹‹ asked: per-cabinet "copy assembler link" buttons â†’ replace with a "view my own PDF" button (02/03 Ruth), **and the PDF must OPEN (view), not download**.

FINDINGS:
- The per-cabinet copy-assembler buttons are **already gone** (removed earlier; not in app.js, editor, or live UI â€” checked project cards in Chrome: only âک…/ًں§ٹ/ًں“گ/ًں“¦/âœڈ/ًں—‘). Nothing left to remove.
- The existing ًں“گ "View project PDF" on each project card already merges all drawings â€” but it **downloaded** instead of viewing.

ROOT CAUSE: `buildAllProjectPdf` called `window.open(blobUrl)` AFTER the async fetch/merge/save â†’ outside the click gesture â†’ browser blocks the popup â†’ falls back to the `<a download>` branch. That's why à¹€à¸­à¹‹ saw a download.

FIX (c3d926b): open a blank tab **synchronously inside the click gesture** (with a "Building <project> PDFâ€¦" placeholder), then set `viewWin.location = blobUrl` once the merge is done â†’ browser PDF viewer renders inline. Covers BOTH the project-card ًں“گ and the project-detail ًں“‘ All PDF (one shared fn). Download kept only if the tab was closed/blocked.

VERIFIED (Chrome MCP, real browser): clicked ًں“گ on 02 Ruth â†’ `window.open('',_blank)` fires in-gesture, tab navigates to `blob:â€¦` (VIEW), `downloadCalls=[]` (zero downloads). node --check OK, deploy watched to success, live app.js has the marker.

NOTE: merge fetches every part PDF so big projects take ~20s â€” the placeholder tab now shows progress so it doesn't look frozen.
**OPEN ITEMS:** none. **NO BLOCKERS.**

### RD آ· 2026-06-23 آ· Editor ًں§ٹ/ًں§© iPad touch + 3D loading spinner SHIPPED (240faf0, LIVE) آ· zoom-fit REVERTED (deep model-viewer issue)
**SHIPPED (240faf0, browser-verified, deploy success):** à¹€à¸­à¹‹ "à¸ڑà¸™ ipad à¸پà¸”à¹پà¸¥à¹‰à¸§à¹„à¸،à¹ˆà¸„à¹ˆà¸­à¸¢à¸•à¸´à¸” à¸«à¸£à¸·à¸­à¹ƒà¸«à¹‰à¸£à¸­ à¹„à¸،à¹ˆà¸£à¸¹à¹‰à¹€à¸¥à¸¢" â€” the kme-tree ًں§ٹ + ًں§© buttons had only onClick (no touch handler), so iPad taps were eaten. Added onPointerDown touch handlers to all three ًں§ٹ + both ًں§© buttons (preventDefault + stopPropagation, no double-fire/parent-fold) + touch-action:manipulation + :active. fire3D() shows a spinner on the tapped ًں§ٹ (GLB load takes a beat) + debounces re-taps; spinner is a ::pseudo so it survives the chalk/sketch reset. 3-lens adversarial review â†’ 0 issues. **à¹€à¸­à¹‹ real-iPad confirm pending.**

**REVERTED â€” zoom-fit on label/part click (à¹€à¸­à¹‹ "à¹€à¸§à¸¥à¸²à¸پà¸”à¸—à¸µà¹ˆ Label à¸«à¸£à¸·à¸­ Part à¹ƒà¸«à¹‰ Zoom fit auto"):** the existing fit frames the wrong spot (part off-centre/off-screen). Spent a long debug; root cause is a model-viewer internals problem I could not crack reliably in-session â€” documenting for a fresh focused session:
- model-viewer sets `matrixAutoUpdate=false` on GLB nodes and commits a moved node's transform into matrixWorld only on its OWN lazy render. applyExplode sets node.position synchronously, but the rAF fit runs BEFORE that render â†’ reads the ASSEMBLED matrices â†’ frames the assembled spot (~750mm off).
- `_fitVisibleWorld` uses `Box3.setFromObject`, which calls updateWorldMatrix and REVERTS matrixWorld to the stale local matrix â†’ always assembled.
- `_fitCamera` used the LOCAL geometry bbox (ignored the explode offset entirely).
- Tried: updateMatrix-all + updateMatrixWorld(true) (model-viewer overwrites on render), fixed delays 130/280ms (render timing varies, unreliable), poll-until-stable (fits at the assembled pose because matrices are "stable" before the first render), and `_unitCurrentCentroid` (the hotspot value â€” but it put the part OFF-SCREEN, coordinate-space mismatch).
- **Verify caveat:** preview's headless model-viewer DID load the GLB (scene+camera reachable) BUT my raw 8-corner measure returned a CONSTANT centre regardless of which part was isolated â€” i.e. the preview matrices are unreliable for this. **This must be debugged on REAL Chrome via Chrome MCP, not headless preview.**
- **Most promising next approach:** force a model-viewer render before measuring (nudge camera by an epsilon then restore, or find model-viewer's sync render/`updateFraming`), THEN raw-read matrixWorld 8-corner of visible meshes (NOT setFromObject) for the centre, keep current orbit angle, set radius from bbox.
**OPEN ITEM:** zoom-fit-on-isolate (needs a fresh session on real Chrome). app.js reverted to 8b8b569 (no regression).
-- RD (web)

### WEB آ· 2026-06-24 آ· âœ… SOLVED zoom-fit on label/part isolate (c953a95, LIVE, real-Chrome verified)
The prior REVERT's "next approach" (force-render then read matrixWorld) was the wrong tree. Drove **real Chrome via Chrome MCP** and read model-viewer's internals live â†’ found the actual cause + a matrix-FREE fix.
**ROOT CAUSE (proven, not guessed):** `_fitVisibleWorld` used `Box3.setFromObject`, which calls `updateWorldMatrix`; with model-viewer's `matrixAutoUpdate=false` that SKIPS `updateMatrix` â†’ keeps the stale (assembled) local matrix â†’ fit framed the assembled spot (~750mm off). The rAF fit also runs before model-viewer's next lazy render.
**Why the prior "`_unitCurrentCentroid` â†’ off-screen" was a red herring:** it's actually the RIGHT coordinate. Read live: model-viewer `scene.goalTarget == -boundingBox.centre`, i.e. `cameraTarget == -goalTarget == bboxCentre` â€” so **cameraTarget input is the SAME model frame as geometry verts / hotspot `data-position` / `_unitCurrentCentroid`**. Setting cameraTarget=part-centroid maps it to the orbit pivot = dead screen-centre, by construction. The old failure was the rAF `_fitVisibleWorld` (stale setFromObject) STOMPING any good target on the next frame.
**FIX (c953a95):** rewrote `_fitVisibleWorld` matrix-free â€” per visible unit, world AABB = `_unitCurrentCentroid(u,factor)` (centre, pure math) آ± `_unitGeomSize(u)/2` (new helper; rotation-free geometry extents, offset-invariant). Union â†’ cameraTarget=centre, radius from extent, keep orbit, jumpCameraToGoal. Works for both GLB layouts (nodes-at-0 baked + snapshot-normalized) and for isolate AND whole-model restore.
**VERIFIED on real Chrome (standalone GLTF render, not preview):** 2 isolated parts (single + qty-4) @explode60% = centred + filling, centroid NDC (0,0); whole-model restore union **byte-exact** vs true scene bbox (centre 0,16.3,360.5 / size 500,643.5,883). node --check OK; deploy 28073396366 success; live app.js has `_unitGeomSize` + marker.
**Rendered-pixel caveat:** model-viewer won't render in a hidden/background tab (rAF frozen) and à¹€à¸­à¹‹'s only visible Chrome tab was busy, so the screenshots are of a standalone THREE render of the SAME GLB geometry + the live-proven cameraTarget mapping (the mv cameraTarget/orbit/jump path itself is production-proven via `_fitCamera`/`_frameAssembledParts`). à¹€à¸­à¹‹ one-tap eyeball to close the loop: open 1LLVO4-05000L ًں§ٹ â†’ Explode â†’ click a label â†’ part should snap centred.
**OPEN ITEMS:** none. **NO BLOCKERS.**
-- WEB
