### WEB آ· 2026-06-24 آ· âœ… zoom-fit STILL off-centre after c953a95 â€” found the REAL cause: cameraTarget axis swap (df0cec4, LIVE, real-render-camera verified)
The prior "âœ… SOLVED" (c953a95) fixed the matrix-staleness half but the part was STILL off-centre / off the bottom. That entry's premise â€” "cameraTarget input is the SAME model frame as geometry verts / hotspot data-position / _unitCurrentCentroid" â€” is **WRONG by a 90آ° rotation**. It was verified against a STANDALONE THREE render, which has NO model-viewer wrapper, so the axis swap was invisible there.
**REAL ROOT CAUSE (proven on real Chrome by projecting through model-viewer's ACTUAL render camera â€” scene.camera â€” not a standalone render):** model-viewer inserts a **-90آ° X rotation (Z-up GLB â†’ Y-up scene) ABOVE the model root** (between Pivot/Target and the GLB `world` node). So:
- geometry verts / node.position / `_unitCurrentCentroid` / hotspot `data-position` live in the GLB **world-LOCAL (Z-up)** frame.
- `mv.cameraTarget` is in model-viewer's **Y-up** frame = `Rx(-90آ°)آ·(world-local)`, i.e. `(x,y,z) â†’ (x, z, -y)`.
Decoded live from the node chain: `matrixWorld(part) = Rx(-90)(worldLocal) + Target.position`, and `Target.position == -cameraTarget`. Feeding the raw world-local centre as cameraTarget aimed ~hundreds of mm off (part at render NDC yâ‰ˆ-1.2, off the bottom) â€” exactly à¹€à¸­à¹‹'s "à¸œà¸´à¸” part / à¸­à¸­à¸پà¸™à¸­à¸پà¸ˆà¸­".
**FIX (df0cec4):** in `_fitVisibleWorld`, after computing the visible-parts world-local bbox centre `(cx,cy,cz)`, rotate it into the viewer frame before assigning cameraTarget: `tx=cx, ty=cz, tz=-cy`. Radius/fov unchanged (rotation preserves extents). One-line frame correction on top of c953a95's matrix-free centre.
**VERIFIED on real Chrome (live site, live exploded model 1LLVO4-05000L @ explode 25%, projected through mv's real render camera):** isolated part's union centre lands at render-camera **NDC (0,0) = dead screen-centre for 5 codes** spanning 1أ—/2أ—/4أ— instances â€” FN0F00, TS2TRX, BXXTR0, DSV2L3, SD00NA â€” each measured after a render-commit (raw matrixWorld 8-corner, not setFromObject). Screenshot of isolated FN0F00 = centred + filling the view. node --check OK; deploy 28073586742 success; live app.js has the `const tx = cx, ty = cz, tz = -cy` marker (curl-confirmed).
**Verify caveat:** after deploy, the live tab's model-viewer wedged on the explode-mode GLB re-fetch loop (`?v=` staleness-poll re-sets src faster than jsdelivr serves it â†’ loaded:false, blank canvas, intermittent CDP renderer freezes). The fix itself was fully verified on the SAME live page+model BEFORE commit (deployed code is byte-identical). à¹€à¸­à¹‹ one-tap eyeball to close the loop: open 1LLVO4-05000L ًں§ٹ â†’ Explode â†’ drag slider ~25% â†’ click a label row â†’ part should snap centred + fill view.
**SIDE NOTE for whoever picks this up:** `_fitCamera` (the manual fit-button + fallback) sets cameraTarget from a world-local bbox centre too and has the SAME missing `Rx(-90)` â€” the whole-model centre sits near the rotation axis so it's only mildly off, but worth the same `(x,z,-y)` fix if à¹€à¸­à¹‹ reports the fit BUTTON mis-aims. Also worth investigating separately: the explode-mode entry GLB-reload thrash (staleness poll re-setting `src` repeatedly â†’ model-viewer never finishes loading â†’ blank canvas) â€” hit it ~3أ— this session.
**OPEN ITEMS:** (1) _fitCamera same axis-swap (low priority, only if fit-button mis-aims). (2) explode-entry GLB-reload thrash (separate bug, intermittent blank canvas). **NO BLOCKERS.**
-- WEB

### WEB آ· 2026-06-24 آ· 3D auto zoom-fit on FIRST open (cb90ee9, LIVE)
à¹€à¸­à¹‹ (screenshot 2FNCL2): à¹€à¸›à¸´à¸” 3D à¸„à¸£à¸±à¹‰à¸‡à¹پà¸£à¸پà¹‚à¸،à¹€à¸”à¸¥à¹„à¸،à¹ˆ fit â€” à¸•à¸¹à¹‰à¹ƒà¸«à¸چà¹ˆà¸«à¸¥à¸¸à¸”à¸،à¸¸à¸،à¸‹à¹‰à¸²à¸¢à¸¥à¹ˆà¸²à¸‡ à¹€à¸«à¹‡à¸™à¹پà¸„à¹ˆà¸،à¸¸à¸،à¹€à¸”à¸µà¸¢à¸§. "à¸پà¹‡à¹ƒà¸«à¹‰ zoom fit à¹€à¸¥à¸¢".
**ROOT:** clean cabinet (no stray bodies) got NO reframe from snapshotScene's outlier filter; worse, for snapshot-NORMALIZED GLBs our geometry-recenter (runs AFTER load) invalidates model-viewer's own auto-frame (camera still targets the pre-recenter centre) â†’ model opens shifted/oversized.
**FIX (cb90ee9):** on the FIRST GLB load (after snapshotScene+applyMode) call `_fitVisibleWorld()` once â€” axis-correct (df0cec4 Rx(-90)) + matrix-free, frames the whole assembled model. **VERIFIED:** node --check OK; deploy 28074336089 success; live app.js has `_didInitialFit` + the `!projectView` guard + fit-buttonâ†’`_fitVisibleWorld` (curl-confirmed). Whole-model framing render (standalone of live 2FNCL2 GLB, 12pc, exact fit formula): centre NDC (0,0), 80% height-fill / 55% width, **no clipping** â€” centred + filling. Centering itself reuses the df0cec4 real-mv-camera-proven `_fitVisibleWorld` mechanism (same fn, all-units set). à¹€à¸­à¹‹ one-tap to close the loop: hard-refresh â†’ open any single cabinet ًں§ٹ â†’ should open already fit/centred (no manual fit needed).
**LESSON applied:** standalone render is valid ONLY for the rotation-invariant fill/radius check; centering correctness comes from the real-mv-camera proof, NOT standalone (that's what made my earlier c953a95 claim false).

### WEB آ· 2026-06-24 آ· "Part à¸پà¸­à¸‡à¸پà¸¥à¸²à¸‡" = STALE GLB â†’ fix: pin 3D URLs to commit SHA (7968568, LIVE)
à¹€à¸­à¹‹ (screenshot 2FCLL0-070043 @0%): "à¹ƒà¸™à¸ڑà¸²à¸‡à¸„à¸£à¸±à¹‰à¸‡ Part à¸،à¸²à¸£à¸§à¸،à¸پà¸±à¸™à¸­à¸¢à¸¹à¹ˆà¸•à¸£à¸‡à¸پà¸¥à¸²à¸‡ à¹پà¸پà¹‰à¹„à¸‚à¸”à¹‰à¸§à¸¢".
**NOT a geometry/web-math bug â€” proven:** scanned ALL 20 cabinet GLBs (local parse, world transforms applied) â†’ every one's parts spread to a proper envelope (2FCLL0-070043 spreadDiag 1413mm; only single-mesh GLBs flag a false "0 spread"). Raw GLB + faithful `snapshotScene` normalization replication both render a CLEAN cabinet. explode/isolate is stateless (derives from immutable baseX). So the pile = a STALE GLB.
**ROOT CAUSE:** `_kd3dGlbUrl` used jsdelivr `@main` + `?v=Date.now()/60000`. jsdelivr caches the `@main` PATH for hours; `?v=` only busts the BROWSER, not jsdelivr's path cache â†’ after a re-export (ًں§ٹ) jsdelivr keeps serving the OLD mis-assembled GLB until its CDN refreshes. à¹€à¸­à¹‹ confirmed a hard-refresh fixed it (= stale, self-heals).
**FIX (7968568):** pin GLB + parts-GLB URLs to the latest **main commit SHA** (`@<sha>`) â€” immutable on jsdelivr â†’ a re-export shows the instant à¹€à¸­à¹‹ reloads, zero CDN lag. SHA fetched once per page load via commits/main; **deliberately NOT cached in sessionStorage** (it survives reloads incl. hard-refresh â†’ would re-pin the OLD sha and reintroduce the bug). Falls back to `@main` until resolved / on rate-limit â†’ 3D never blocks.
**VERIFIED (real Chrome, live):** deployed app.js has `_kd3dGlbRef()`/`_kd3dInitGlbRef`, URLs use `@${_kd3dGlbRef()}`; runtime `window.__KD_GLB_REF` = valid 40-hex SHA (7968568da8â€¦); `_kd3dGlbUrl('2FCLL0-070043')` â†’ `@7968568da8â€¦`; `@<sha>` GLB â†’ HTTP200 valid spread=1413; `@main` fallback â†’ 200. node --check OK; deploy 28076208170 success.
**FYI Fusion (RD):** this only masks staleness on the WEB read side. If you want re-exports visible WITHOUT à¹€à¸­à¹‹ reloading, a jsdelivr purge (`https://purge.jsdelivr.net/gh/<repo>@main/Drawings/3d/<code>.glb`) after the ًں§ٹ push would refresh the `@main` path too. Not required â€” the @sha pin makes reload reliable.
**OPEN ITEMS:** none. **NO BLOCKERS.**
-- WEB

### WEB آ· 2026-06-24 آ· 3D viewer UI overhaul â€” static alpha labels + header buttons (431e131â†’cc5ea11, LIVE)

(2FCLL0-070043: 9 codes â†’ 5L/4R, 2BK001â€¦2CN002 | 2CN026â€¦2UPLL0.)

### WEB آ· 2026-06-24 آ· ًںژ¯ REAL root cause of "parts pile in centre" FOUND + FIXED (29dbc3a, LIVE)
à¹€à¸­à¹‹ frustrated â€” pile kept coming back on the latest code. **My earlier "stale GLB cache" diagnosis was WRONG.** Found the true cause on real Chrome with a decisive test.
**ROOT CAUSE:** model-viewer sets `node.matrixAutoUpdate=false` on the GLB nodes AFTER its first render (a VISIBLE tab). snapshotScene's normalization recenters each part's geometry to origin and sets `node.position` â€” but with matrixAutoUpdate=false a bare `position.set` NEVER reaches `matrixWorld` (THREE skips updateMatrix), so every part renders at the SAME matrixWorld point = pile. Intermittent ("à¹ƒà¸™à¸ڑà¸²à¸‡à¸„à¸£à¸±à¹‰à¸‡") = whether matrixAutoUpdate was already flipped when snapshotScene ran. On a HIDDEN tab it never flips (stays true) so the pile doesn't reproduce there â€” which is why my standalone renders looked clean and I mis-blamed cache.
**DECISIVE TEST (real Chrome):** with matrixAutoUpdate=false, moving 6 nodes to distinct positions â†’ matrixWorld ALL identical (-350,-574,185). Calling `updateMatrix()` per node â†’ matrixWorld spreads (-350,-150,50,250,450,650). Then full normalization + matrixAutoUpdate=false + the fix â†’ matrixWorld = 14 UNIQUE positions, envelope 700أ—425أ—1200, rendered = clean cabinet.
**FIX (29dbc3a):** `node.updateMatrix()` after EVERY `node.position.set` â€” snapshotScene normalize, applyExplode, resetExplode â€” + `matrixAutoUpdate=true` on each unit as insurance. Deterministic, not a race.
**ALSO:** close button `margin-left:auto` â†’ pinned far-RIGHT (à¹€à¸­à¹‹ "à¸›à¸´à¸”à¸«à¸™à¹‰à¸²à¸ˆà¸­ à¹ƒà¸«à¹‰à¸­à¸¢à¸¹à¹ˆà¸،à¸¸à¸،à¸‚à¸§à¸²" â€” it sat mid-header on wide/mobile). Verified top-right.
**Note:** the @sha GLB pin (7968568) is still a good improvement (kills genuinely-stale CDN GLBs) but it was NOT what fixed the pile â€” this matrix fix is. Memory corrected ([[reference_modelviewer_projection]] + [[reference_pages_cache_busting]]).
**OPEN ITEMS:** none. **NO BLOCKERS.**
-- WEB

---
### WEB آ· 2026-06-24 آ· ًںژ¯ pile FULLY FIXED â€” needed updateMatrixWorld too (b0ba6bc, end-to-end verified)
Follow-up to 29dbc3a: `updateMatrix()` alone did NOT fix it (à¹€à¸­à¹‹ still saw the pile). Diagnosed on the DEPLOYED code via dispatched-load under matrixAutoUpdate=false: each node's LOCAL matrix was correct (elements[12] spread -318/23/364) BUT `matrixWorld` (what renders) stayed all-identical (-350,-574,185) = pile â€” model-viewer's render does NOT propagate our matrix edits to matrixWorld.
**THE missing piece:** `explodeRoot.updateMatrixWorld(true)` after the position loops (applyExplode + resetExplode). Forcing it on the live piled state â†’ matrixWorld 14 unique, envelope 682أ—1174أ—393.
**END-TO-END VERIFIED on deployed b0ba6bc:** dispatched the real load handler under matrixAutoUpdate=false â†’ snapshotScene+applyExplode ran â†’ matrixWorld = 14 unique spread + rendered a CLEAN cabinet. (Before: 1 identical = pile.)
**Full fix = updateMatrix() (local matrix) + explodeRoot.updateMatrixWorld(true) (â†’ matrixWorld) after every position change.** Memory corrected ([[reference_modelviewer_projection]]).
**OPEN ITEMS:** none. **NO BLOCKERS.**
-- WEB

### WEB آ· 2026-06-24 آ· ًںژ¯ "2nd open piles" root cause = model-viewer parse-cache reuse â†’ fresh-per-open (c8c09d4, LIVE)
à¹€à¸­à¹‹ nailed it: "à¸پà¸”à¸„à¸£à¸±à¹‰à¸‡à¹پà¸£à¸پà¸–à¸¹à¸پ à¸„à¸£à¸±à¹‰à¸‡à¸—à¸µà¹ˆ 2 part à¸،à¸²à¸£à¸§à¸،à¸پà¸±à¸™ ... à¸­à¸¢à¹ˆà¸²à¹€à¸­à¸² cache à¹€à¸”à¸´à¸،à¸،à¸²à¹ƒà¸ٹà¹‰." TRUE cause: model-viewer's CachingGLTFLoader caches the PARSED scene by URL and shares geometry across instances; snapshotScene normalization recenters the geometry verts IN PLACE â†’ the 1st open mutates the cache-shared geometry â†’ the 2nd open of the same cabinet gets already-recentered geometry â†’ all parts collapse to origin = pile. (My @sha pin made URLs stable, which is what let the cache hit on re-open â€” masking it as "intermittent" before.)
**FIX (c8c09d4):** (1) per-OPEN nonce `window.__KD_OPEN_NONCE` (bumped at the top of `_kdOpen3D`) appended as `&o=` in `_kd3dGlbUrl`/`_kd3dPartsGlbUrl` â†’ every open is a unique URL â†’ model-viewer parses FRESH (provably no cache reuse). (2) clone geometry before recentering in snapshotScene so the cache is never corrupted even if hit.
**VERIFIED (real Chrome):** 3 consecutive opens â†’ nonce 4/5/6, 3 UNIQUE GLB URLs (cache-key differs â†’ fresh parse each), @sha still present. This is provable (cache keyed by URL) â€” doesn't need reproducing the render. node --check OK; deploy success.
**Trade-off:** re-fetch+re-parse per open (jsdelivr edge-cached @sha path â†’ fast). Correctness > the small reload cost (à¹€à¸­à¹‹'s call).
**OPEN ITEMS:** none. **NO BLOCKERS.**
-- WEB

### WEB آ· 2026-06-24 آ· ًں”§ HOTFIX "Loading 3Dâ€¦ à¹„à¸،à¹ˆà¸‚à¸¶à¹‰à¸™" â€” my own regression from moving close âœ• to the viewer (ff8d0c7)
à¹€à¸­à¹‹: 3D view stuck on "Loading 3Dâ€¦" forever. **My regression (bebc453):** I moved the close âœ• into the VIEWER template (bottom-right), but the close-handler `modal.querySelector('.kdstock-close').addEventListener('click', close)` runs in the EARLY modal setup â€” BEFORE the viewer template is built (it's built after `await _kd3dEnsureModelViewer()` + body.innerHTML). So `.kdstock-close` was null there â†’ `null.addEventListener` threw â†’ _kdOpen3D aborted before building the viewer â†’ stuck. (GLB=200, model-viewer CDN loads â€” both fine; my earlier bebc453 "verify" used an INJECTED element, which bypassed this real-flow crash â†’ lesson: verify the actual open flow, not injected DOM.)
**FIX (ff8d0c7):** optional-chain the early handler (`?.` â†’ no crash; backdrop+Esc still close from there) + wire the floating close AFTER the viewer builds (next to the fit-button wiring). 
**VERIFIED on the real flow (real Chrome):** open 2FNCL2 â†’ errs=[] (no null crash), bodyHasViewer=true (viewer DOM built, was the 105-char Loading placeholder), model-viewer defined+present, close âœ• at bottom-right. node --check OK; deploy 28088666955 success. (Model pixel-render is hidden-CDP-tab-gated; builds + renders on à¹€à¸­à¹‹'s visible tab.)

### WEB آ· 2026-06-24 آ· ًں”ڑ SESSION HANDOFF (3D viewer marathon) â†’ fresh WEB session
**3D viewer (`_kdOpen3D` in app.js) â€” all LIVE today.** Latest HEAD has: explode-only (no mode bar); auto zoom-fit on first open + fit button (4-corner icon) moved BOTTOM-LEFT; close âœ• = dark circle BOTTOM-RIGHT; dims 3-line W/D/H (inherit colour); static alphabetical labels split equal L/R; bigger explode slider (40px thumb); GLB URLs pinned `@<latest main sha>` + per-open `&o=` nonce; "3D"/fullscreen buttons removed.
**PILE saga â€” RESOLVED (à¹€à¸­à¹‹ confirmed clean on her browser). TWO independent causes, both fixed:** (1) model-viewer sets `matrixAutoUpdate=false` post-render â†’ bare `node.position.set` never reaches `matrixWorld` â†’ all parts at one spot. Fix = `updateMatrix()` + `explodeRoot.updateMatrixWorld(true)` after every position change. (2) model-viewer's parse-cache shares geometry across opens; snapshotScene recenters geometry IN PLACE â†’ 2nd open of same cabinet reused mutated geometry â†’ pile. Fix = per-open `&o=` (unique URL â†’ fresh parse) + `clone()` geometry before recentering. See [[reference_modelviewer_projection]].
**LAST fix:** "Loading 3Dâ€¦ à¹„à¸،à¹ˆà¸‚à¸¶à¹‰à¸™" (ff8d0c7) = close-wiring regression (moved âœ• to viewer template but its handler ran in early setup â†’ null crash â†’ _kdOpen3D aborted). Fixed: `?.` + wire after viewer builds.
**âڑ  VERIFICATION RULES (I got burned 3أ—):** (a) HIDDEN CDP tabs don't render model-viewer (WebGL rAF gated) AND often won't even BUILD the viewer (model-viewer module load gated) â†’ can't pixel-verify 3D from a background tab; (b) read `node.matrixWorld.elements[12..14]`, NOT node.position (position â‰  what renders); (c) verify the REAL open flow, not injected DOM or standalone THREE renders (a standalone render missed model-viewer's -90آ° wrapper rotation; an injected close-float missed the real-flow crash); (d) à¹€à¸­à¹‹'s visible tab is the only reliable pixel confirm â†’ give her a 1-tap repro. (e) GitHub API unauth rate-limit (60/hr) got exhausted to 0 by many test page-loads (each fires commits/main + mtime probes) â€” be sparing.
**OPEN ITEMS:** (1) à¹€à¸­à¹‹ to confirm post-ff8d0c7: loading works + open/close/re-open same cabinet stays clean. (2) `&o=` re-downloads the GLB each open (jsdelivr edge-cached â†’ fast; revisit only if opens feel slow). (3) dblclick-modelâ†’fullscreen still wired (à¸›à¸¸à¹ˆà¸، fullscreen removed; à¹€à¸­à¹‹ hasn't asked to kill the gesture).

### WEB (RD 09) آ· 2026-06-26 آ· phantom "cabinet-as-part" dropped (1NSVB0-060050 false ERR) + **NEEDS Fusion root fix**
**à¹€à¸­à¹‹ flagged:** Nest row #2 "1NSVB0-060050" shows as a part with NO DXF (the "â›” 1 ERR") but it's a CABINET (group, ~17 children, no sheet-metal body). Confirmed via walk_debug line 198 (depth-0, no SM, no material) + manifest (variant_root===self, no parent_code, no is_wrapper, qty 1).
**ROOT CAUSE (Fusion, CC_Assembly.py):** leaf-vs-container is gated on MATERIAL alone â€” `design_alpf = 'alpf' in mat` (~:645). 1NSVB0's root carries an ALPF tag but owns NO sheet-metal body, so it took the LEAF path (:679), got a master urn + qty 1 + NO is_wrapper, bypassing the container/is_wrapper record (:676). The `sm` flag (scanner._is_sheet_metal) is computed (:638) but only used for diagnostics. **The OLD "re-ًں”¥ to export its DXF" belief is WRONG** â€” it has no body, no flat/DXF can ever exist.
**WEB FIX (71d003e, LIVE-verified):** SAFE 4-clause backstop in `_loadProjectParts` â€” drop a code iff (a) never a child, (b) variant_root===self, (c) parents â‰¥1 other part, (d) NO DXF. Clause (d) = hard fail-safe â†’ real dual-role parts (e.g. **SD00NA-080050**, a side panel that also parents SD0SUP supports) are NEVER dropped. Workflow verified across 131 projects: drops EXACTLY 1NSVB0-060050. `console.warn` on drop. Verified live (Chrome preview): 04 Ruth 54â†’53 unique, 53/53 DXF, "1 ERR" gone, SD00NA + BSRUTH retained.
**NEEDS (Fusion / RD-Fusion):** fix the root in CC_Assembly so containers are flagged is_wrapper by SHEET-METAL BODY, not material. Proposed (workflow): gate container branch `if has_kids and not (sm and (design_alpf or cam_alpf))` (:676) + leaf branch `if (design_alpf or cam_alpf) and (sm or not has_kids)` (:679); compute has_kids before :679. âڑ  confirm live first: 1NSVB0-060050's root has an ALPF body with ZERO sheet-metal features (no flatPattern/flange/bend + no sheetMetalRules) before landing. After the Fusion fix + a CC_Assembly re-scan, 1NSVB0 emits is_wrapper:true and the web guard becomes a no-op for it. âڑ  a manifest-only heuristic canNOT separate phantom from a real SD00NA in projects where SD00NA has no parent_code â€” only the SM-body gate (Fusion) can; the web guard is correct there only because clause (d) protects DXF-backed parts.
-- WEB

### WEB (RD 09) آ· 2026-06-26 آ· True Shape nest OVERLAP fix (DSV1TR triangle) â€” needs live eyeball
**à¹€à¸­à¹‹ flagged:** MODE "True Shape" placed `DSV1TR-060050` (a 254-pt curved 'triangle') OVERLAPPING a neighbour on sheet 4 (à¸‹à¹‰à¸­à¸™à¸پà¸±à¸™ = both parts ruined). Confirmed REAL: empirical polygon-cross check on the BEFORE layout found 1 real overlap (DSV1TR أ— DSV2B3-070031-Ruth) matching à¹€à¸­à¹‹'s screenshot.
**ROOT CAUSE:** `_rasterMask` (nest.js:2246) sampled each grid row at its CENTRE Y only â†’ diagonal/curved edges left edge cells uncovered â†’ mask UNDER-approximated the true shape â†’ a neighbour packed into the diagonal gap â†’ true outlines overlapped. (Rectangles unaffected â€” centre sampling is exact for axis-aligned.)
**FIX (f7944f2):** sample each row at TOP+CENTRE+BOTTOM, union x-spans (over-cover diagonals) + dilate the mask 1 cell on NON-rectangular parts only (rects stay tight). Mask now âٹ‡ true shape â†’ the packer's grid collision GUARANTEES no overlap. Packing no-regression (04 Ruth True Shape 89 placed/0 unplaced/4 sheets).
**âڑ  NOT visually verified:** the sheet is a `<canvas>` (no extractable geometry) and my automated polygon check mis-reads DSV1TR's 254-pt curved outline (overlap count rose as masks GREW = impossible for real overlaps â†’ detector unreliable for this part), and the headless canvas renders too small to eyeball. The fix is provably-correct by the maskâٹ‡true-shape invariant, but **needs an eyeball on the LIVE render** (à¹€à¸­à¹‹'s browser). Guaranteed-safe fallback meanwhile: **Desktop mode** (bounding-box, physically can't overlap).
-- WEB

### WEB (RD 09) آ· 2026-06-26 آ· True Shape overlap DEEP-DIVE + 4-sheet packing answer (à¹€à¸­à¹‹ delegated)
**OUTCOME for à¹€à¸­à¹‹'s goal "fit all on 4 sheets":**
- **4أ— 10x5 (3050أ—1525) = 15,400 is the ONLY/optimum 4-sheet solution** (Desktop, all 89 placed, 0 unplaced, **0 overlap verified**).
- **4-foot ("40*4") sheets are GEOMETRICALLY IMPOSSIBLE:** `BSRUTH-000000` = 2464أ—**1256** mm; its short side 1256 > 1220 â†’ cannot lie flat on ANY 4-foot sheet, any rotation. Plus the rest needs 5â€“6 sheets: 10x4â†’5 sheets+BSRUTH unplaced; 8x4â†’6 sheets+BSRUTH unplaced.
- **â†’ Recommend à¹€à¸­à¹‹ use DESKTOP mode** (bbox pack, physically can't overlap; verified 0 overlap) for cutting. It meets the 4-sheet goal at the optimum.
**True Shape overlap (DSV1TR-060050) â€” REAL, deep-dived, NOT yet fixed:**
- Confirmed real via a true-polygon edge-cross detector whose transform I proved IDENTICAL to the renderer `_drawSheet` transform (4968) AND the mask `mapPt` (2256). Detector validated: Desktop run = 0 overlaps (provably overlap-free).
- DSV1TR's outer is a VALID simple polygon (254 pts, 0 self-intersections, area 202402mmآ²). Its raster mask (3-edge-sample+dilate) was verified to COVER 100% of its true shape (1692 interior samples, 0 uncovered, mask 218808 > true 202402mmآ²).
- Despite correct masks + matching transforms + grid placement, the packer still places ~5 neighbours overlapping DSV1TR on sheet 4 â†’ **the bug is in the COLLISION LOGIC (_blFind/_stamp/pack loop), NOT the mask.** My commit f7944f2 (conservative mask) attacked the wrong layer and did NOT fix it â†’ **REVERTED (bfb7b61).**
- NEXT (open): instrument the live raster packer (_blFind/_stamp) to catch why a non-clashing grid placement renders as a true-shape overlap â€” needs a focused debugging session. Meanwhile Desktop is the safe path.
-- WEB

### WEB (RD 09) آ· 2026-06-26 آ· Common-line cutting (à¸£à¸§à¸،à¹€à¸ھà¹‰à¸™) â€” opt-in, verified (à¹€à¸­à¹‹ requested)
**Feature (fa2806f):** where two parts touch on a STRAIGHT edge, cut that edge ONCE (saves material + cut length). Opt-in **ًں”— Common-line** toggle next to Gap/Manual + a **tab** sub-toggle (+mm) for uncut micro-bridges so common-cut parts don't shift mid-cut. à¹€à¸­à¹‹ confirmed: laser does kerf-comp; rectangles/straight edges only.
**How:** when ON, `_buildSheetDxf` buffers axis-aligned OUTER edges â†’ `_commonLineMerge` dedups coincident/overlapping collinear spans (cut once; spans shared by â‰¥2 parts optionally broken with ~tabMm uncut bridges, ~1 per 250mm) â†’ emits. Curves/diagonals/holes/border/labels untouched. **Default OFF â†’ _buildSheetDxf byte-identical when off (zero regression).** Best with Desktop + Gap 0 (MaxRects places rects edge-to-edge exactly).
**VERIFIED (preview, _test hooks buildSheetDxf/commonLineMerge):** (1) engine unit tests â€” sharedâ†’1 line, partial overlapâ†’split, separateâ†’unchanged, long shared+tabsâ†’broken with bridges. (2) **controlled e2e**: two rects sharing a 50mm edge â†’ OFF outerCut=600mm, ON=550mm (edge cut once = âˆ’50), ON+tabs=550mm (edge split w/ uncut bridge). (3) UI toggle wires+persists+enables tab control. node --check âœ“.
**Dataset note:** 04 Ruth (53 varied unique parts) has ~no coincident straight edges (only ~6 near-shared, tiny overlaps) â†’ ~0 saving there. Common-line pays off on rectangle-heavy nests (many identical panels). à¹€à¸­à¹‹ to test on a rectangle-heavy job + confirm on a scrap cut before production (cut-order/tab safety is the operator's call when tabs off).
-- WEB

### WEB (RD 09) آ· 2026-06-26 آ· âœ… FIXED True Shape overlap â€” flip180 baked into pack (root cause found)
**à¹€à¸­à¹‹ flagged 4أ—.** ROOT CAUSE (finally pinned via live `_test.rasterMask` instrumentation, after a per-minute-cache stale-code detour that masked it): the **FLIP180 post-pack pass** (was nest.js ~4209) rotated every `flip180` placement **+180آ° IN PLACE after packing**. The "180 never swaps w/h â†’ same slot" assumption is true for the BBOX but FALSE for the SHAPE â€” a non-rect part (`DSV1TR-060050` triangle, the only flip180 part among the 5 overlaps) rotated 180آ° moves to the opposite bbox corner â†’ overlaps neighbours the packer interlocked against its ORIGINAL orientation. The raster pack output `r` was CLEAN; the post-pass injected the overlap â†’ a collision/mask check on `r` never caught it (proven: masks correct + cover their shapes, yet 256-cell clash at the post-flip positions).
**FIX (0ee133c):** True-Shape raster packer now BAKES flip180 into the packed orientation â€” reserve the mask for `rot+180`, place at `rot+180`, mark `_flipBaked`. Post-pass skips `_flipBaked` (still applies to bbox modes, where flip-in-place is safe). + belt-and-suspenders `_resultHasTrueOverlap`â†’Desktop fallback in `_nestMultiSheet`.
**VERIFIED live (04 Ruth, fresh code, polygon-cross detector whose transform == renderer):** True Shape overlaps **5 â†’ 0**; Desktop 0; 89 placed / 0 unplaced / 4 sheets (no regression).
**Lesson:** the per-minute `?v=` cache-bust + my rapid same-minute reloads served STALE nest.js â†’ my conservative-mask attempt was never actually run â†’ I wrongly concluded "didn't work". Cross the minute (or hard-reload) before judging a preview build. (feedback_open_tab_stale_code.)
-- WEB
